---
title: "STAT 544 - SWITRS Crash Severity Notebook (Rebuilt for Correctly Merged Data)"
output:
  html_document:
    df_print: paged
---

# Package Chunk
```{r setup, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# What: Global setup (packages, options, theme).
# Why: Ensures reproducibility and consistent formatting across the notebook.

options(stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
set.seed(544)

required_pkgs <- c(
  "dplyr", "tidyr", "stringr", "ggplot2", "scales", "purrr",
  "readr", "forcats", "corrplot", "broom", "rlang"
)

installed <- rownames(installed.packages())
to_install <- setdiff(required_pkgs, installed)
if (length(to_install) > 0) install.packages(to_install)

library(rlang)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(stringr)
library(ggplot2)
library(scales)
library(purrr)
library(readr)
library(forcats)
library(corrplot)
library(broom)

theme_set(theme_minimal(base_size = 12))
```

# Load data (correctly merged dataset)
```{r}
# What: Load crash-grain merged SWITRS dataset (1 row per CASE_ID).
# Why: Parties/Victims have already been aggregated to crash-level in the merge notebook.

na_tokens <- c("NA", "N/A", "NULL", "null")

switrs_raw <- read.csv("merged_fixed_switrs.csv", na.strings = na_tokens, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

switrs <- switrs_raw
rm(switrs_raw)

dim(switrs)
glimpse(switrs)
```

# Basic sanity checks
```{r}
# What: Confirm crash-grain (1 row per crash).
# Why: The merge notebook output should already be 1 row per CASE_ID.

stopifnot("CASE_ID" %in% names(switrs))
stopifnot(n_distinct(switrs$CASE_ID) == nrow(switrs))

# Optional: show a few IDs to verify
head(switrs$CASE_ID, 10)
```

# Standardize "not stated" / placeholder missingness (do not lump into OTHER)
```{r}
# What: Normalize placeholder encodings with SWITRS-aware rules.
# Why: Standardizes "-" and blank encodings across the crash-grain dataset.
#      Preserves SWITRS semantics for "Y or blank" flags (blank/NA means No).

trim_chr <- function(x) stringr::str_trim(as.character(x))

# Global helper used later (EDA labels, hypothesis tests, plots)
# NOTE: we default blank_to_notstated=TRUE, but in the cleaning step below
#       we set blank_to_notstated=FALSE so Y-or-blank flags can still become "N".
normalize_not_stated <- function(x, not_stated_label = "Not Stated", blank_to_notstated = TRUE) {
  x <- trim_chr(x)

  # dashes and explicit "not stated"
  x[x %in% c("-", " -", "- ", " - ")] <- not_stated_label
  x[toupper(x) %in% c("NOT STATED", "NOTSTATED")] <- not_stated_label

  # optionally treat blank as Not Stated
  if (isTRUE(blank_to_notstated)) {
    x[x == ""] <- not_stated_label
  }
  x
}

# SWITRS "Y or blank" flags: blank (and often NA after read.csv) means NO
y_or_blank_flags <- intersect(
  c("PEDESTRIAN_ACCIDENT","BICYCLE_ACCIDENT","MOTORCYCLE_ACCIDENT","TRUCK_ACCIDENT",
    "NOT_PRIVATE_PROPERTY","ALCOHOL_INVOLVED"),
  names(switrs)
)

# 1) Trim + normalize dash-style placeholders on all character fields
#    IMPORTANT: do NOT convert blank->Not Stated here; we handle blanks in steps 2/3
switrs_clean <- switrs %>%
  mutate(across(where(is.character), ~ normalize_not_stated(.x, blank_to_notstated = FALSE)))

# 2) For most character fields: "" => "Not Stated" (do NOT touch y-or-blank flags here)
char_cols <- names(switrs_clean)[sapply(switrs_clean, is.character)]
other_char_cols <- setdiff(char_cols, y_or_blank_flags)

switrs_clean <- switrs_clean %>%
  mutate(across(all_of(other_char_cols), ~ {
    x <- .x
    x[x == ""] <- "Not Stated"
    x
  }))

# 3) For y-or-blank flags: NA/"" => "N" (blank means No); keep "Not Stated" if it exists
switrs_clean <- switrs_clean %>%
  mutate(across(all_of(y_or_blank_flags), ~ {
    x <- trim_chr(.x)
    x[is.na(x) | x == ""] <- "N"
    x[x == "Y"] <- "Y"
    x
  }))

# Free memory: switrs no longer needed after switrs_clean exists
rm(switrs)
```

# Covert "UNKNOWN" road names to NA (special case)
```{r}
# What: Convert literal UNKNOWN / Not Stated for roadway name fields to NA.
# Why: For roadway names, these behave like missingness (not meaningful categories).

road_name_vars <- intersect(c("PRIMARY_RD", "SECONDARY_RD"), names(switrs_clean))

switrs_clean <- switrs_clean %>%
  mutate(across(all_of(road_name_vars), ~ {
    x <- as.character(.x)
    x <- str_trim(x)
    x[toupper(x) == "UNKNOWN"] <- NA_character_
    x[x == "Not Stated"] <- NA_character_
    x
  }))
```

# Convert Y/N and Y-only flags carefully
```{r}
# What: Standardize common flag formats into consistent factors.
# Why: SWITRS includes Y/N/blank-not-stated fields AND Y-or-blank fields (blank = No).

# Convert the "Y or blank" crash flags into factor(N, Y, Not Stated)
y_or_blank_flags <- intersect(
  c("PEDESTRIAN_ACCIDENT","BICYCLE_ACCIDENT","MOTORCYCLE_ACCIDENT","TRUCK_ACCIDENT",
    "NOT_PRIVATE_PROPERTY","ALCOHOL_INVOLVED"),
  names(switrs_clean)
)

switrs_clean <- switrs_clean %>%
  mutate(across(all_of(y_or_blank_flags), ~ {
    x <- str_trim(as.character(.x))
    # SWITRS: blank means No
    x[is.na(x) | x == ""] <- "N"
    # Dash was already normalized to "Not Stated" earlier
    x[x == "Y"] <- "Y"
    factor(x, levels = c("N","Y","Not Stated"))
  }))

# Standard Y/N/Not Stated fields (e.g., INTERSECTION, STATE_HWY_IND, TOW_AWAY, AT_FAULT, etc.)
crash_yn_whitelist <- intersect(
  c("INTERSECTION","STATE_HWY_IND","TOW_AWAY","AT_FAULT"),  # add others we *intend*
  names(switrs_clean)
)

switrs_clean[crash_yn_whitelist] <- lapply(switrs_clean[crash_yn_whitelist], function(x) {
  x <- str_trim(as.character(x))
  x[x %in% c("", NA)] <- "Not Stated"
  factor(x, levels = c("N","Y","Not Stated"))
})

# HIT_AND_RUN is F/M/N (+ Not Stated), not Y/N.
if ("HIT_AND_RUN" %in% names(switrs_clean)) {
  switrs_clean <- switrs_clean %>%
    mutate(HIT_AND_RUN = factor(
      ifelse(is.na(HIT_AND_RUN), "Not Stated", HIT_AND_RUN),
      levels = c("N","M","F","Not Stated")
    ))
}

# PCF_VIOL_CATEGORY is Char(2): keep leading zeros (01, 02, ..., 24, 00). 
if ("PCF_VIOL_CATEGORY" %in% names(switrs_clean)) {
  switrs_clean <- switrs_clean %>%
    mutate(
      PCF_VIOL_CATEGORY = as.character(PCF_VIOL_CATEGORY),
      PCF_VIOL_CATEGORY = str_trim(PCF_VIOL_CATEGORY),
      PCF_VIOL_CATEGORY = ifelse(PCF_VIOL_CATEGORY == "Not Stated", "Not Stated",
                                 ifelse(PCF_VIOL_CATEGORY %in% c("", NA), NA_character_, PCF_VIOL_CATEGORY)),
      PCF_VIOL_CATEGORY = ifelse(
        !is.na(PCF_VIOL_CATEGORY) & PCF_VIOL_CATEGORY != "Not Stated" & str_detect(PCF_VIOL_CATEGORY, "^[0-9]+$"),
        str_pad(PCF_VIOL_CATEGORY, width = 2, pad = "0"),
        PCF_VIOL_CATEGORY
      )
    )
}
```

# Build the crash-level analysis table
```{r}
# What: Finalize crash-level analysis table.
# Why: Data are already crash-grain; after cleaning we proceed with switrs_reduced.

switrs_reduced <- switrs_clean
rm(switrs_clean)
```

# validate Road_Surface
```{r}
# What: Validate ROAD_SURFACE against codebook-valid codes.
# Why: Codebook defines ROAD_SURFACE as A-D (plus Not Stated). Anything else is out-of-spec.

if ("ROAD_SURFACE" %in% names(switrs_reduced)) {

  valid_road_surface <- c("A","B","C","D","Not Stated")

  switrs_reduced <- switrs_reduced %>%
    mutate(
      # normalize blanks/dashes/etc to "Not Stated" (and treat NA as Not Stated for coded fields)
      ROAD_SURFACE = normalize_not_stated(ROAD_SURFACE, blank_to_notstated = TRUE),
      ROAD_SURFACE = ifelse(is.na(ROAD_SURFACE), "Not Stated", ROAD_SURFACE),

      ROAD_SURFACE_invalid = ifelse(ROAD_SURFACE %in% valid_road_surface, 0L, 1L),
      ROAD_SURFACE_raw = ifelse(ROAD_SURFACE_invalid == 1L, ROAD_SURFACE, NA_character_),

      # recode invalid codes to Not Stated
      ROAD_SURFACE = ifelse(ROAD_SURFACE_invalid == 1L, "Not Stated", ROAD_SURFACE)
    )

  cat("Out-of-spec ROAD_SURFACE codes (flagged + recoded to Not Stated):\n")
  print(table(switrs_reduced$ROAD_SURFACE_raw, useNA = "ifany"))
}
```

# Cleanup: remove ROAD_SURFACE validation helper columns (data-quality flags)
```{r}
switrs_reduced <- switrs_reduced %>%
  dplyr::select(-dplyr::any_of(c("ROAD_SURFACE_invalid","ROAD_SURFACE_raw")))
```


# Missingness Diagnostics (before dropping columns)
```{r}
# What: Missingness diagnostics at the correct crash-level grain.
# Why: NA% on merged rows is misleading (structural NA from join).

missing_na_pct <- colMeans(is.na(switrs_reduced)) * 100

notstated_pct <- sapply(switrs_reduced, function(x) {
  if (!(is.character(x) || is.factor(x))) return(NA_real_)
  mean(as.character(x) == "Not Stated", na.rm = TRUE) * 100
})

missing_tbl <- tibble::tibble(
  variable = names(switrs_reduced),
  pct_na = as.numeric(missing_na_pct),
  pct_notstated = as.numeric(notstated_pct)
) %>%
  arrange(desc(pct_na), desc(pct_notstated))

head(missing_tbl, 30)
```

# drop columns with too much true NA
```{r}
# What: Drop columns exceeding NA threshold (crash-level only).
# Why: Now NA% reflects real missingness, not merge-structure missingness.

high_miss_threshold <- 30  # percent NA (tune if needed)

missing_na_pct <- colMeans(is.na(switrs_reduced)) * 100
high_miss_vars <- names(missing_na_pct)[missing_na_pct > high_miss_threshold]

# Never drop core outcome/impact fields, even if NA% is high
protect_vars <- intersect(c("COLLISION_SEVERITY", "NUMBER_KILLED", "NUMBER_INJURED"), names(switrs_reduced))
high_miss_vars <- setdiff(high_miss_vars, protect_vars)

length(high_miss_vars)
head(high_miss_vars, 50)

switrs_reduced <- switrs_reduced %>%
  select(-all_of(high_miss_vars))

dim(switrs_reduced)
```

# drop deprecated / non-analytic variables
```{r}
# What: Remove variables deprecated in the codebook or not analytically useful.

drop_vars <- intersect(
  c("CHP_SHIFT","BEAT_TYPE","CHP_BEAT_CLASS","CALTRANS_COUNTY","CALTRANS_DISTRICT","STATE_ROUTE",
    "ROUTE_SUFFIX","POSTMILE_PREFIX","POSTMILE","LOCATION_TYPE","RAMP_INTERSECTION","CHP_ROAD_TYPE"),
  names(switrs_reduced)
)

switrs_reduced <- switrs_reduced %>% select(-all_of(drop_vars))
dim(switrs_reduced)
```

# Preserve raw-coded severity and create SevereBinary outcome
```{r}
# Preserve raw-coded severity and create outcomes (KSI + fatal-only + deaths)
# What: Create SevereBinary (KSI proxy) from COLLISION_SEVERITY, plus FatalBinary/Deaths for impact reporting.
# Why: SevereBinary (codes 1/2) is the main actionable “prevent worst outcomes” target.
#      FatalBinary/Deaths keep the story explicitly “save lives” without leaking into predictors.

# --- Severity code (authoritative KSI proxy) ---
if ("COLLISION_SEVERITY" %in% names(switrs_reduced)) {
  switrs_reduced <- switrs_reduced %>%
    mutate(
      COLLISION_SEVERITY_CODE = str_trim(as.character(COLLISION_SEVERITY)),
      COLLISION_SEVERITY_CODE = ifelse(COLLISION_SEVERITY_CODE %in% c("", "Not Stated"), NA_character_, COLLISION_SEVERITY_CODE),

      # Primary outcome (KSI): fatal(1) or severe injury(2)
      SevereBinary = case_when(
        COLLISION_SEVERITY_CODE %in% c("1","2") ~ "Severe/Fatal",   # KSI
        COLLISION_SEVERITY_CODE %in% c("0","3","4") ~ "Other",
        TRUE ~ NA_character_
      ),
      SevereBinary = factor(SevereBinary, levels = c("Other","Severe/Fatal"))
    )
} else {
  stop("COLLISION_SEVERITY not found; cannot create SevereBinary/KSI.")
}

# --- Fatal-only + deaths (for impact ranking / sensitivity) ---
if ("NUMBER_KILLED" %in% names(switrs_reduced)) {
  switrs_reduced <- switrs_reduced %>%
    mutate(
      NUMBER_KILLED_NUM = suppressWarnings(as.integer(NUMBER_KILLED)),
      FatalBinary = case_when(
        is.na(NUMBER_KILLED_NUM) ~ NA_integer_,
        NUMBER_KILLED_NUM > 0 ~ 1L,
        TRUE ~ 0L
      ),
      # Deaths as a numeric metric (keep NA as NA; summaries should use na.rm=TRUE)
      Deaths = NUMBER_KILLED_NUM
    )
} else {
  switrs_reduced$FatalBinary <- NA_integer_
  switrs_reduced$Deaths <- NA_integer_
}

cat("\nOutcome counts:\n")
print(table(switrs_reduced$SevereBinary, useNA = "ifany"))

# --- Optional audit: do fatality counts disagree with severity=1? ---
if (all(c("FatalBinary","COLLISION_SEVERITY_CODE") %in% names(switrs_reduced))) {
  sev_fatal_code <- switrs_reduced$COLLISION_SEVERITY_CODE == "1"
  cat("\nAudit table: COLLISION_SEVERITY==1 (fatal code) vs FatalBinary(NUMBER_KILLED>0)\n")
  print(table(sev_fatal_code, switrs_reduced$FatalBinary, useNA = "ifany"))

  mismatch <- sum(sev_fatal_code & switrs_reduced$FatalBinary == 0, na.rm = TRUE) +
              sum(!sev_fatal_code & switrs_reduced$FatalBinary == 1, na.rm = TRUE)
  cat("Fatal-code vs killed-count mismatches (non-NA):", mismatch, "\n")
}
```

# Leak-proofing: ban outcome/harm variables from ever being used as predictors
```{r}
# Leak-proofing: ban outcome/harm variables from ever being used as predictors
leakage_vars <- c(
  # main outcomes / labels
  "COLLISION_SEVERITY", "COLLISION_SEVERITY_CODE", "COLLISION_SEVERITY_LAB",
  "SevereBinary",

  # fatal/death metrics (should NEVER be predictors)
  "NUMBER_KILLED", "NUMBER_KILLED_NUM", "FatalBinary", "Deaths",

  # injury counts (harm)
  "NUMBER_INJURED",
  "COUNT_SEVERE_INJ", "COUNT_VISIBLE_INJ",
  "COUNT_PED_KILLED", "COUNT_PED_INJURED",
  "COUNT_BICYCLIST_KILLED", "COUNT_BICYCLIST_INJURED",
  "COUNT_MC_KILLED", "COUNT_MC_INJURED",

  # victim-derived “severity-ish” aggregates (harm/leakage)
  "n_victims_killed", "any_victim_ksi"
)

leakage_vars <- intersect(leakage_vars, names(switrs_reduced))
cat("Leakage vars detected in data (will be banned from predictors):\n")
print(leakage_vars)
```

# Location hotspots
```{r}
# WHERE: Hotspot ranking (severe/fatal rate) using available location fields
# What: Create a crash-location key (best available), then rank locations by severe/fatal rate.
# Why: Converts the analysis into a resource-allocation tool: "where to focus first."

has_latlon <- all(c("LATITUDE", "LONGITUDE") %in% names(switrs_reduced))
has_roads  <- all(c("PRIMARY_RD", "SECONDARY_RD") %in% names(switrs_reduced))
has_city   <- "CITY" %in% names(switrs_reduced)

switrs_where <- switrs_reduced %>%
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(SevereBinary)) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    # A) Lat/Long bin (~0.01°) for coarse hotspot screening
    lat_bin = if (has_latlon) round(as.numeric(LATITUDE), 2) else NA_real_,
    lon_bin = if (has_latlon) round(as.numeric(LONGITUDE), 2) else NA_real_,
    latlon_key = if (has_latlon) paste0(lat_bin, ", ", lon_bin) else NA_character_,

    # B) Intersection proxy: normalize road strings
    rd1 = if (has_roads) stringr::str_squish(toupper(as.character(PRIMARY_RD))) else NA_character_,
    rd2 = if (has_roads) stringr::str_squish(toupper(as.character(SECONDARY_RD))) else NA_character_,

    rd1 = dplyr::if_else(is.na(rd1) | rd1 %in% c("", "NOT STATED", "UNKNOWN"), NA_character_, rd1),
    rd2 = dplyr::if_else(is.na(rd2) | rd2 %in% c("", "NOT STATED", "UNKNOWN"), NA_character_, rd2),

    # IMPORTANT: order the pair per-row so A&B == B&A 
    rd_lo = dplyr::if_else(!is.na(rd1) & !is.na(rd2), pmin(rd1, rd2), NA_character_),
    rd_hi = dplyr::if_else(!is.na(rd1) & !is.na(rd2), pmax(rd1, rd2), NA_character_),
    inter_key = dplyr::if_else(!is.na(rd_lo) & !is.na(rd_hi), paste0(rd_lo, " & ", rd_hi), NA_character_),

    # C) City fallback
    city_key = if (has_city) {
      x <- stringr::str_squish(toupper(as.character(CITY)))
      dplyr::if_else(is.na(x) | x %in% c("", "NOT STATED", "UNKNOWN"), NA_character_, x)
    } else NA_character_,

    # Final location key preference
    location_key = dplyr::case_when(
      !is.na(inter_key) ~ inter_key,
      is.na(inter_key) & !is.na(latlon_key) ~ latlon_key,
      is.na(inter_key) & is.na(latlon_key) & !is.na(city_key) ~ city_key,
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    ),
    location_type = dplyr::case_when(
      !is.na(inter_key) ~ "Intersection (PRIMARY_RD & SECONDARY_RD)",
      is.na(inter_key) & !is.na(latlon_key) ~ "Lat/Lon bin (~0.01°)",
      is.na(inter_key) & is.na(latlon_key) & !is.na(city_key) ~ "City",
      TRUE ~ "Missing"
    )
  ) %>%
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(location_key))

# Rank hotspots by Severe/Fatal rate with a minimum denominator
min_crashes <- 50

hotspot_tbl <- switrs_where %>%
  dplyr::group_by(location_type, location_key) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(
    n_crashes = dplyr::n(),
    severe_n = sum(SevereBinary == "Severe/Fatal", na.rm = TRUE),
    severe_per_100 = 100 * severe_n / n_crashes,
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  dplyr::filter(n_crashes >= min_crashes) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(dplyr::desc(severe_per_100), dplyr::desc(n_crashes))

# WHERE: Make hotspot output usable (rate + count views)

min_crashes <- 10   # better for intersection-grain
min_severe  <- 1    # require at least 1 severe/fatal to be a "hotspot" by rate

hotspot_tbl2 <- switrs_where %>%
  group_by(location_type, location_key) %>%
  summarise(
  n_crashes = n(),
  severe_n = sum(SevereBinary == "Severe/Fatal", na.rm = TRUE),
  severe_per_100 = 100 * severe_n / n_crashes,
  fatal_crash_n = sum(FatalBinary == 1, na.rm = TRUE),
  deaths = sum(Deaths, na.rm = TRUE),
  deaths_per_100 = 100 * deaths / n_crashes,
  .groups = "drop"
)

# A) Hotspots by RATE (with minimum volume + at least 1 severe)
hotspots_by_rate <- hotspot_tbl2 %>%
  filter(n_crashes >= min_crashes, severe_n >= min_severe) %>%
  arrange(desc(severe_per_100), desc(n_crashes))

cat("\nTop 20 hotspots by Severe/Fatal RATE (min_crashes =", min_crashes,
    ", min_severe =", min_severe, "):\n")
print(head(hotspots_by_rate, 20))

# B) Hotspots by COUNT (largest severe burden)
hotspots_by_count <- hotspot_tbl2 %>%
  filter(n_crashes >= min_crashes) %>%
  arrange(desc(severe_n), desc(n_crashes))

# C) Hotspots by DEATHS
hotspots_by_deaths <- hotspot_tbl2 %>%
  filter(n_crashes >= min_crashes) %>%
  arrange(desc(deaths), desc(n_crashes))

cat("\nTop 20 hotspots by DEATHS (min_crashes =", min_crashes, "):\n")
print(head(hotspots_by_deaths, 20))

cat("\nTop 20 hotspots by Severe/Fatal COUNT (min_crashes =", min_crashes, "):\n")
print(head(hotspots_by_count, 20))
```

# Sanity Check
```{r}
dplyr::count(switrs_where, location_type)
summary(nchar(switrs_where$location_key))
```

# Derive core time features(+ engineered risk flags & interactions)
```{r}
# What: Derive standardized time features (year, hour, weekend, time_bucket) and
#       engineer proposal-aligned risk flags + interaction terms.
# Why:  (1) Time features directly answer the client’s “when should we focus?” question.
#       (2) Engineered flags make coded SWITRS fields interpretable (e.g., rain, dark, DUI, speeding).
#       (3) Interaction terms operationalize “high-risk conditions” (e.g., Night×DUI, Rain×Speeding,
#           Pedestrian×Dark) which supports targeted enforcement/interventions rather than generic summaries.
#       (4) All engineered features are non-leakage (they do not use injury counts or fatalities).

safe_parse_yyyymmdd <- function(x) {
  # What: Parse YYYYMMDD strings to Date.
  # Why: SWITRS dates often come as numeric-like strings; we need robust Date objects for features/plots.
  x <- as.character(x)
  x <- str_trim(x)
  x[x %in% c("", "Not Stated")] <- NA_character_
  suppressWarnings(as.Date(x, format = "%Y%m%d"))
}

safe_parse_hhmm <- function(x) {
  # What: Parse HHMM (military time) and return hour (0–23).
  # Why: Hour-of-day is one of the highest-value features for operational planning.
  x <- as.character(x)
  x <- str_trim(x)
  x[x %in% c("", "Not Stated")] <- NA_character_
  x_num <- suppressWarnings(as.integer(x))
  x_num[x_num >= 2500] <- NA_integer_   # SWITRS unknown time codes 2500–2559
  hr <- x_num %/% 100
  hr[hr < 0 | hr > 23] <- NA_integer_
  hr
}

# ---- Core time features ----
# What: Create year from ACCIDENT_YEAR.
# Why: Supports year-to-year comparison and protects against mixed-year files.
if ("ACCIDENT_YEAR" %in% names(switrs_reduced)) {
  switrs_reduced <- switrs_reduced %>% mutate(year = as.integer(ACCIDENT_YEAR))
}

# What: Create hour (0–23) from COLLISION_TIME.
# Why: Allows time-of-day risk profiling and time-bucket aggregation.
if ("COLLISION_TIME" %in% names(switrs_reduced)) {
  switrs_reduced <- switrs_reduced %>% mutate(hour = safe_parse_hhmm(COLLISION_TIME))
}

# What: Create weekend indicator from DAY_OF_WEEK.
# Why: Weekend vs weekday is a key policy/operations split (staffing, patrol emphasis).
if ("DAY_OF_WEEK" %in% names(switrs_reduced)) {
  switrs_reduced <- switrs_reduced %>%
    mutate(
      dow_chr = as.character(DAY_OF_WEEK),
      weekend = case_when(
        dow_chr %in% c("6","7","Saturday","Sunday") ~ 1L,
        dow_chr %in% c("1","2","3","4","5","Monday","Tuesday","Wednesday","Thursday","Friday") ~ 0L,
        TRUE ~ NA_integer_
      ),
      weekend = factor(weekend, levels = c(0,1), labels = c("Weekday","Weekend"))
    ) %>%
    select(-dow_chr)
}

# What: Create interpretable time buckets from hour.
# Why: Makes results client-friendly and reduces noise vs. 24 separate hour levels.
if ("hour" %in% names(switrs_reduced)) {
  switrs_reduced <- switrs_reduced %>%
    mutate(
      time_bucket = case_when(
        is.na(hour) ~ NA_character_,
        hour >= 0 & hour <= 5 ~ "Late Night (12–5)",
        hour >= 6 & hour <= 9 ~ "AM Commute (6–9)",
        hour >= 10 & hour <= 15 ~ "Midday (10–3)",
        hour >= 16 & hour <= 19 ~ "PM Commute (4–7)",
        TRUE ~ "Evening (8–11)"
      ),
      time_bucket = factor(time_bucket, levels = c(
        "Late Night (12–5)","AM Commute (6–9)","Midday (10–3)",
        "PM Commute (4–7)","Evening (8–11)"
      ))
    )
}

# ---- Engineered risk flags + interactions (proposal-aligned) ----
# What: Check column availability before engineering features.
# Why: Keeps the notebook robust if the merged file has slightly different columns.
has_pcf      <- "PCF_VIOL_CATEGORY" %in% names(switrs_reduced)
has_weather  <- "WEATHER_1" %in% names(switrs_reduced)
has_light    <- "LIGHTING" %in% names(switrs_reduced)
has_ped      <- "PEDESTRIAN_ACCIDENT" %in% names(switrs_reduced)
has_hour     <- "hour" %in% names(switrs_reduced)

switrs_reduced <- switrs_reduced %>%
  mutate(
    # What: Night indicator (20:00–05:59).
    # Why: “Night driving” risk patterns are different and are central to many interventions.
    night = if (has_hour) {
      case_when(
        is.na(hour) ~ NA_integer_,
        hour %in% c(20:23, 0:5) ~ 1L,
        TRUE ~ 0L
      )
    } else NA_integer_,

    # What: PCF-based DUI flag (01 = DUI; 23 = Ped/Other DUI).
    # Why: Provides an interpretable DUI-related risk proxy that can be interacted with time/lighting.
    pcf_dui = if (has_pcf) {
      x <- as.character(PCF_VIOL_CATEGORY)
      x <- str_trim(x)
      case_when(
        x %in% c("01","23") ~ 1L,
        x %in% c("Not Stated", "", NA) ~ NA_integer_,
        TRUE ~ 0L
      )
    } else NA_integer_,

    # What: PCF-based unsafe speed flag (03).
    # Why: Speeding is a common actionable lever (enforcement, signage, engineering).
    pcf_speed = if (has_pcf) {
      x <- as.character(PCF_VIOL_CATEGORY)
      x <- str_trim(x)
      case_when(
        x == "03" ~ 1L,
        x %in% c("Not Stated", "", NA) ~ NA_integer_,
        TRUE ~ 0L
      )
    } else NA_integer_,

    # What: Rain indicator from WEATHER_1 (C = Raining).
    # Why: Weather is a situational condition; “rainy days” is explicitly in the client prompt.
    is_rain = if (has_weather) {
      x <- as.character(WEATHER_1)
      x <- str_trim(x)
      case_when(
        x == "C" ~ 1L,
        x %in% c("Not Stated", "", NA) ~ NA_integer_,
        TRUE ~ 0L
      )
    } else NA_integer_,

    # What: Dark indicator from LIGHTING (C/D/E = dark variants).
    # Why: Lighting is strongly operational (street lighting, patrol visibility, nighttime enforcement).
    is_dark = if (has_light) {
      x <- as.character(LIGHTING)
      x <- str_trim(x)
      case_when(
        x %in% c("C","D","E") ~ 1L,
        x %in% c("Not Stated", "", NA) ~ NA_integer_,
        TRUE ~ 0L
      )
    } else NA_integer_,

    # What: Pedestrian-involved indicator from PEDESTRIAN_ACCIDENT.
    # Why: “Pedestrian involved” is a major severity driver and aligns with proposal emphasis.
    is_ped = if (has_ped) {
      x <- as.character(PEDESTRIAN_ACCIDENT)
      x <- str_trim(x)
      case_when(
        x == "Y" ~ 1L,
        x == "N" ~ 0L,
        x %in% c("Not Stated", "", NA) ~ NA_integer_,
        TRUE ~ NA_integer_
      )
    } else NA_integer_,

    # What: Interaction terms capturing compounded risk.
    # Why: These are the most “consulting-grade” outputs: not just whether factors matter,
    #      but which *combinations* create the highest-risk scenarios.
    night_dui  = ifelse(!is.na(night) & !is.na(pcf_dui),     as.integer(night == 1L & pcf_dui == 1L), NA_integer_),
    rain_speed = ifelse(!is.na(is_rain) & !is.na(pcf_speed), as.integer(is_rain == 1L & pcf_speed == 1L), NA_integer_),
    ped_dark   = ifelse(!is.na(is_ped) & !is.na(is_dark),    as.integer(is_ped == 1L & is_dark == 1L), NA_integer_)
  ) %>%
  mutate(across(
    c(night, pcf_dui, pcf_speed, is_rain, is_dark, is_ped, night_dui, rain_speed, ped_dark),
    ~ factor(.x, levels = c(0,1), labels = c("No","Yes"))
  ))
```

# HYPOTHESIS TEST (ONE-SIDED): Weekend has higher Severe/Fatal rate than Weekday
```{r}
# What: Test whether the Severe/Fatal proportion is higher on weekends vs weekdays.
# Why: This answers the directional policy question (“which is worse?”) using a formal one-sided test.

if (!all(c("weekend", "SevereBinary") %in% names(switrs_reduced))) {
  stop("Need 'weekend' and 'SevereBinary' in switrs_reduced. Run the feature engineering + outcome chunks first.")
}

df_wk <- switrs_reduced %>%
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(weekend), !is.na(SevereBinary)) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    weekend = as.character(weekend),
    is_severe = (SevereBinary == "Severe/Fatal")
  ) %>%
  dplyr::filter(weekend %in% c("Weekend", "Weekday"))

# Counts
n_weekend <- sum(df_wk$weekend == "Weekend")
n_weekday <- sum(df_wk$weekend == "Weekday")

x_weekend <- sum(df_wk$is_severe[df_wk$weekend == "Weekend"], na.rm = TRUE)
x_weekday <- sum(df_wk$is_severe[df_wk$weekend == "Weekday"], na.rm = TRUE)

p_weekend <- x_weekend / n_weekend
p_weekday <- x_weekday / n_weekday
diff_pp   <- 100 * (p_weekend - p_weekday)

cat("\n============================================================\n")
cat("ONE-SIDED TWO-PROPORTION TEST: Weekend > Weekday\n")
cat("============================================================\n")

cat("\nH0: p_weekend <= p_weekday\n")
cat("HA: p_weekend  > p_weekday\n")

cat("\nGroup summaries:\n")
cat("  Weekend: n =", n_weekend, "| severe =", x_weekend, "| p̂ =", round(p_weekend, 4), "\n")
cat("  Weekday: n =", n_weekday, "| severe =", x_weekday, "| p̂ =", round(p_weekday, 4), "\n")
cat("\nDifference (Weekend - Weekday) =", round(diff_pp, 3), "percentage points\n")

# One-sided prop test (approx z-test; no continuity correction)
test_wk <- suppressWarnings(
  prop.test(
    x = c(x_weekend, x_weekday),
    n = c(n_weekend, n_weekday),
    alternative = "greater",
    correct = FALSE
  )
)

cat("\nTest statistic (X-squared):", round(unname(test_wk$statistic), 4), "\n")
cat("One-sided p-value:", signif(test_wk$p.value, 5), "\n")

if (!is.na(test_wk$p.value) && test_wk$p.value < 0.05) {
  cat("\nConclusion (alpha=0.05): Reject H0. Weekends have a HIGHER Severe/Fatal rate than weekdays.\n")
} else {
  cat("\nConclusion (alpha=0.05): Fail to reject H0. Not enough evidence that weekends are higher.\n")
}
```

# Hypothesis testing continued: Severe/Fatal vs Other for all predictors (or as many as possible)
```{r}
# What: Run appropriate tests for every column vs SevereBinary.
# Why: Professor request: hypothesis tests for all variables (or as many as we can).

# ----------------------------
# Configuration knobs
# ----------------------------
max_levels <- 60     # skip categorical predictors with > this many unique levels
chisq_B    <- 2000   # simulation reps when expected counts are small
min_n_per_group <- 10
verbose_per_var <- TRUE   # TRUE = print blocks like your screenshot; FALSE = summary only

if (!exists("switrs_reduced")) stop("switrs_reduced not found.")
if (!("SevereBinary" %in% names(switrs_reduced))) stop("SevereBinary not found in switrs_reduced.")
if (!exists("leakage_vars")) leakage_vars <- character(0)

# ----------------------------
# Choose variables: ALL columns except outcome/leakage and obvious IDs
# ----------------------------
exclude_always <- c(
  "SevereBinary",
  "CASE_ID",
  "COLLISION_SEVERITY",
  "COLLISION_SEVERITY_CODE",
  "FatalBinary","Deaths","NUMBER_KILLED","NUMBER_KILLED_NUM","NUMBER_INJURED"
)

vars_all <- setdiff(names(switrs_reduced), unique(c(exclude_always, leakage_vars)))
cat("Total candidate variables to test:", length(vars_all), "\n")

# ----------------------------
# Label rownames for printing ONLY (does not change counts/levels)
# ----------------------------
label_rows_if_available <- function(var_name, tab) {
  if (exists("legend_maps") && exists("lookup_meaning") && var_name %in% names(legend_maps)) {
    rn <- rownames(tab)
    rownames(tab) <- lookup_meaning(rn, legend_maps[[var_name]])
  }
  tab
}

# ----------------------------
# Main test runner (one variable)
# ----------------------------
test_one_var_all <- function(var_name, data = switrs_reduced, outcome = "SevereBinary") {

  df <- data %>%
    dplyr::select(all_of(c(outcome, var_name))) %>%
    dplyr::filter(!is.na(.data[[outcome]]))

  df[[outcome]] <- factor(df[[outcome]], levels = c("Other","Severe/Fatal"))
  x <- df[[var_name]]

  # Skip all-missing
  if (all(is.na(x))) {
    if (verbose_per_var) {
      cat("\n============================================================\n")
      cat("VARIABLE:", var_name, "\n")
      cat("============================================================\n")
      cat("SKIP: All missing\n")
    }
    return(tibble::tibble(variable = var_name, type = "NA", test = "SKIP",
                          p_value = NA_real_, p_value_alt = NA_real_,
                          n_used = 0L, note = "All missing"))
  }

  # Treat numeric 0/1 (or 2 unique values) as categorical
  if (is.numeric(x) || is.integer(x)) {
    ux <- unique(x[is.finite(x)])
    if (length(ux) <= 2) x <- as.factor(x)
  }

  # -------------------------
  # NUMERIC: Welch t-test + Wilcoxon (FIXED)
  # -------------------------
  if (is.numeric(x) || is.integer(x)) {
    df2 <- df %>%
      dplyr::mutate(x_num = as.numeric(.data[[var_name]])) %>%
      dplyr::filter(is.finite(x_num))

    n_other <- sum(df2[[outcome]] == "Other")
    n_sev   <- sum(df2[[outcome]] == "Severe/Fatal")

    if (verbose_per_var) {
      cat("\n============================================================\n")
      cat("VARIABLE:", var_name, "\n")
      cat("============================================================\n")
      cat("Type: numeric\n")
      cat("H0: mean(x|Severe/Fatal) = mean(x|Other)\n")
      cat("HA: means differ\n\n")
      cat("Group means:\n")
      cat("  Other:       n =", n_other, " mean =", round(mean(df2$x_num[df2[[outcome]]=="Other"]), 4), "\n")
      cat("  Severe/Fatal:n =", n_sev,   " mean =", round(mean(df2$x_num[df2[[outcome]]=="Severe/Fatal"]), 4), "\n\n")
    }

    if (n_other < min_n_per_group || n_sev < min_n_per_group) {
      if (verbose_per_var) cat("SKIP: Too few observations in one group\n")
      return(tibble::tibble(variable = var_name, type = "numeric", test = "SKIP",
                            p_value = NA_real_, p_value_alt = NA_real_,
                            n_used = nrow(df2), note = "Too few observations in one group"))
    }

    # FIX: create a real grouping column for formula methods
    df2$g <- df2[[outcome]]

    tt <- tryCatch(t.test(x_num ~ g, data = df2, var.equal = FALSE), error = function(e) NULL)
    wx <- tryCatch(wilcox.test(x_num ~ g, data = df2, exact = FALSE), error = function(e) NULL)

    if (verbose_per_var) {
      cat("Welch t-test p-value:", if (!is.null(tt)) signif(tt$p.value, 6) else "NA", "\n")
      cat("Wilcoxon p-value:    ", if (!is.null(wx)) signif(wx$p.value, 6) else "NA", "\n")
    }

    return(tibble::tibble(
      variable = var_name,
      type = "numeric",
      test = "Welch t-test (+ Wilcoxon)",
      p_value = if (!is.null(tt)) tt$p.value else NA_real_,
      p_value_alt = if (!is.null(wx)) wx$p.value else NA_real_,
      n_used = nrow(df2),
      note = NA_character_
    ))
  }

  # -------------------------
  # CATEGORICAL: 2-level prop.test; 3+ chi-square
  # -------------------------
  x_chr <- normalize_not_stated(x, blank_to_notstated = TRUE)
  df2 <- df %>% dplyr::mutate(x = x_chr) %>% dplyr::filter(!is.na(x))

  k <- length(unique(df2$x))
  if (k <= 1) {
    if (verbose_per_var) {
      cat("\n============================================================\n")
      cat("VARIABLE:", var_name, "\n")
      cat("============================================================\n")
      cat("SKIP: Constant after filtering\n")
    }
    return(tibble::tibble(variable = var_name, type = "categorical", test = "SKIP",
                          p_value = NA_real_, p_value_alt = NA_real_,
                          n_used = nrow(df2), note = "Constant after filtering"))
  }
  if (k > max_levels) {
    if (verbose_per_var) {
      cat("\n============================================================\n")
      cat("VARIABLE:", var_name, "\n")
      cat("============================================================\n")
      cat("SKIP: High cardinality (", k, " levels > ", max_levels, ")\n", sep = "")
    }
    return(tibble::tibble(variable = var_name, type = "categorical", test = "SKIP",
                          p_value = NA_real_, p_value_alt = NA_real_,
                          n_used = nrow(df2), note = paste0("High cardinality (", k, " levels > ", max_levels, ")")))
  }

  tab <- table(df2$x, df2[[outcome]], useNA = "no")

  if (verbose_per_var) {
    cat("\n============================================================\n")
    cat("VARIABLE:", var_name, "\n")
    cat("============================================================\n")
    cat("Type: categorical\n\n")
    tab_print <- label_rows_if_available(var_name, tab)
    cat("Contingency table (rows = predictor, cols = outcome):\n")
    print(addmargins(tab_print))
    cat("\n")
  }

  # 2-level => two-proportion test
  if (nrow(tab) == 2) {
    x_succ <- tab[, "Severe/Fatal"]
    n_tot  <- rowSums(tab)
    pt <- suppressWarnings(prop.test(x = x_succ, n = n_tot, correct = FALSE))

    if (verbose_per_var) cat("Two-proportion test p-value:", signif(pt$p.value, 6), "\n")

    return(tibble::tibble(
      variable = var_name,
      type = "categorical (2 levels)",
      test = "Two-proportion test (prop.test)",
      p_value = pt$p.value,
      p_value_alt = NA_real_,
      n_used = sum(tab),
      note = NA_character_
    ))
  }

  # 3+ levels => chi-square; simulate if small expected
  cs0 <- suppressWarnings(chisq.test(tab))
  use_sim <- any(cs0$expected < 5)
  cs <- if (use_sim) suppressWarnings(chisq.test(tab, simulate.p.value = TRUE, B = chisq_B)) else cs0

  if (verbose_per_var) cat("Chi-square p-value:", signif(cs$p.value, 6), if (use_sim) " (sim)\n" else "\n")

  tibble::tibble(
    variable = var_name,
    type = paste0("categorical (", nrow(tab), " levels)"),
    test = if (use_sim) paste0("Chi-square (sim p, B=", chisq_B, ")") else "Chi-square",
    p_value = cs$p.value,
    p_value_alt = NA_real_,
    n_used = sum(tab),
    note = NA_character_
  )
}

# ----------------------------
# Run all tests + summary (like previous version)
# ----------------------------
results_all <- purrr::map_dfr(vars_all, ~ test_one_var_all(.x))

results_all <- results_all %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    p_adj_fdr = p.adjust(p_value, method = "BH")
  ) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(p_adj_fdr, p_value)

cat("\n\n==================== SUMMARY (sorted by FDR) ====================\n")
print(head(results_all, 50))

cat("\nHow many were skipped?\n")
print(table(results_all$test, useNA = "ifany"))

cat("\nTop skip reasons:\n")
print(results_all %>% dplyr::filter(test == "SKIP") %>% dplyr::count(note, sort = TRUE) %>% head(20))
```

# Month and seasonality panel (feature engineering and storyboard-ready rate plot)
```{r}
# TIME: Robust collision_date parsing + month seasonality panel (REPLACE ENTIRE CHUNK)
# What: Parse COLLISION_DATE safely, then derive month features and summarize Severe/Fatal rate by month.
# Why: Avoids all-NA date parsing failures and produces a single storyboard-ready seasonality panel.

if (!("COLLISION_DATE" %in% names(switrs_reduced))) {
  stop("COLLISION_DATE not found in switrs_reduced.")
}

# Robust parser for common SWITRS export formats
parse_date_multi <- function(x) {
  x <- as.character(x)
  x <- trimws(x)
  x[x %in% c("", "Not Stated", "UNKNOWN", "NA", "N/A", "NULL", "null")] <- NA_character_

  d1 <- suppressWarnings(as.Date(x, format = "%Y%m%d"))     # 20240123
  d2 <- suppressWarnings(as.Date(x, format = "%Y-%m-%d"))   # 2024-01-23
  d3 <- suppressWarnings(as.Date(x, format = "%m/%d/%Y"))   # 01/23/2024

  out <- d1
  out[is.na(out)] <- d2[is.na(out)]
  out[is.na(out)] <- d3[is.na(out)]
  out
}

# Create/overwrite collision_date
switrs_reduced$collision_date <- parse_date_multi(switrs_reduced$COLLISION_DATE)

cat("collision_date parsed % = ",
    round(mean(!is.na(switrs_reduced$collision_date)) * 100, 2), "%\n", sep = "")

# Derive month features
switrs_reduced <- switrs_reduced %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    month = as.integer(format(collision_date, "%m")),
    month_name = factor(month.abb[month], levels = month.abb, ordered = TRUE)
  )

# Month severity-rate table
month_tbl <- switrs_reduced %>%
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(SevereBinary), !is.na(month_name)) %>%
  dplyr::group_by(month_name) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(
    n_crashes = dplyr::n(),
    severe_n = sum(SevereBinary == "Severe/Fatal", na.rm = TRUE),
    severe_per_100 = 100 * severe_n / n_crashes,
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(month_name)

cat("Severe/Fatal rate per 100 crashes by month:\n")
print(month_tbl)

# Single storyboard-ready plot
ggplot(month_tbl, aes(x = month_name, y = severe_per_100)) +
  geom_col() +
  geom_text(aes(label = round(severe_per_100, 2)), vjust = -0.3, size = 3.5) +
  labs(
    title = "Severe/Fatal Rate by Month",
    x = "Month",
    y = "Severe/Fatal per 100 crashes"
  ) +
  theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank())
```

# Statistics report chunk: Hypothesis tests on Severe/Fatal proportions (week 8 deliverable)
```{r}
# What: Run two-proportion tests for Severe/Fatal rate differences across key groups.
# Why: Produces a formal hypothesis test artifact aligned with the course requirement and client decisions.

# ---- Helper: run a two-proportion test with a clean printed report ----
two_prop_report <- function(data, group_var, group_a, group_b,
                            outcome_var = "SevereBinary",
                            outcome_success = "Severe/Fatal",
                            title = NULL) {

  if (!all(c(group_var, outcome_var) %in% names(data))) {
    stop("Missing required columns for test: ", group_var, " and/or ", outcome_var)
  }

  df <- data %>%
    dplyr::select(all_of(c(group_var, outcome_var))) %>%
    dplyr::filter(!is.na(.data[[group_var]]), !is.na(.data[[outcome_var]])) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      g = as.character(.data[[group_var]]),
      y = (.data[[outcome_var]] == outcome_success)
    ) %>%
    dplyr::filter(g %in% c(group_a, group_b))

  n_a <- sum(df$g == group_a)
  n_b <- sum(df$g == group_b)
  x_a <- sum(df$y[df$g == group_a], na.rm = TRUE)
  x_b <- sum(df$y[df$g == group_b], na.rm = TRUE)

  p_a <- x_a / n_a
  p_b <- x_b / n_b
  diff <- p_a - p_b

  test <- suppressWarnings(stats::prop.test(x = c(x_a, x_b), n = c(n_a, n_b), correct = FALSE))
  ci <- test$conf.int
  pval <- test$p.value

  if (is.null(title)) title <- paste0(outcome_success, " proportion: ", group_a, " vs ", group_b)

  cat("\n============================================================\n")
  cat("STATISTICS REPORT —", title, "\n")
  cat("============================================================\n")

  cat("\nH0: p(", group_a, ") = p(", group_b, ")\n", sep = "")
  cat("HA: p(", group_a, ") ≠ p(", group_b, ")\n", sep = "")

  cat("\nGroup summaries:\n")
  cat("  ", group_a, ": n = ", n_a, ", severe = ", x_a,
      ", p̂ = ", round(p_a, 4), "\n", sep = "")
  cat("  ", group_b, ": n = ", n_b, ", severe = ", x_b,
      ", p̂ = ", round(p_b, 4), "\n", sep = "")

  cat("\nEstimated difference (p̂A − p̂B) = ", round(diff, 4), "\n", sep = "")
  cat("95% CI for (pA − pB): [", round(ci[1], 4), ", ", round(ci[2], 4), "]\n", sep = "")
  cat("Two-proportion test p-value: ", signif(pval, 4), "\n", sep = "")

  if (!is.na(pval) && pval < 0.05) {
    cat("\nInterpretation: Reject H0 at α = 0.05. The Severe/Fatal rate differs between groups.\n")
  } else {
    cat("\nInterpretation: Fail to reject H0 at α = 0.05. No statistically significant difference detected.\n")
  }

  invisible(list(
    group_a = group_a, group_b = group_b,
    n = c(n_a, n_b), x = c(x_a, x_b),
    p = c(p_a, p_b), diff = diff,
    ci = ci, p_value = pval
  ))
}

# ---- Test 1: Weekend vs Weekday ----
if ("weekend" %in% names(switrs_reduced)) {
  two_prop_report(
    data = switrs_reduced,
    group_var = "weekend",
    group_a = "Weekend",
    group_b = "Weekday",
    title = "Weekend vs Weekday"
  )
} else {
  cat("\nSkipping Test 1: 'weekend' not found.\n")
}

# ---- Test 2: Rain vs Clear (WEATHER_1) ----
# Codebook: WEATHER_1 A=Clear, C=Raining
if ("WEATHER_1" %in% names(switrs_reduced)) {

  df_weather <- switrs_reduced %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      WEATHER_1 = trimws(as.character(WEATHER_1)),
      WEATHER_1 = dplyr::case_when(
        WEATHER_1 %in% c("A","C") ~ WEATHER_1,
        TRUE ~ NA_character_
      )
    )

  two_prop_report(
    data = df_weather,
    group_var = "WEATHER_1",
    group_a = "C",   # Raining
    group_b = "A",   # Clear
    title = "Raining (WEATHER_1=C) vs Clear (WEATHER_1=A)"
  )

} else {
  cat("\nSkipping Test 2: 'WEATHER_1' not found.\n")
}

# ---- Test 3: Night×DUI proxy (interaction) vs not ----
# Uses engineered factor night_dui = Yes/No if our time-feature chunk ran.
if ("night_dui" %in% names(switrs_reduced)) {

  df_nd <- switrs_reduced %>%
    dplyr::mutate(night_dui = as.character(night_dui)) %>%
    dplyr::filter(night_dui %in% c("Yes","No"))

  two_prop_report(
    data = df_nd,
    group_var = "night_dui",
    group_a = "Yes",
    group_b = "No",
    title = "Night × DUI proxy (night_dui=Yes) vs Not (night_dui=No)"
  )

} else {
  cat("\nSkipping Test 3: 'night_dui' not found (re-run the time-feature chunk if needed).\n")
}
```

# Statistics report (add-on):Dark vs Daylight, Alcohol vs No Alcohol, Rain×Speeding
```{r}
# What: Add two-proportion tests tied to operational decisions and compound-risk conditions.
# Why: These map directly to interventions (night visibility, DUI enforcement, wet-weather speed control).

# Reuse two_prop_report() from prior chunk
if (!exists("two_prop_report")) stop("two_prop_report() not found. Run the main Statistics Report chunk first.")

# ---- Test 4: Dark vs Daylight (LIGHTING) ----
# Codebook: A=Daylight, B=Dusk/Dawn, C/D/E=Dark variants (exclude B for clean contrast)
if ("LIGHTING" %in% names(switrs_reduced)) {

  df_light <- switrs_reduced %>%
    dplyr::filter(!is.na(SevereBinary)) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      LIGHTING = trimws(as.character(LIGHTING)),
      light_group = dplyr::case_when(
        LIGHTING == "A" ~ "Daylight",
        LIGHTING %in% c("C","D","E") ~ "Dark",
        TRUE ~ NA_character_
      )
    )

  two_prop_report(
    data = df_light,
    group_var = "light_group",
    group_a = "Dark",
    group_b = "Daylight",
    title = "Dark (LIGHTING=C/D/E) vs Daylight (LIGHTING=A)"
  )

} else {
  cat("\nSkipping Test 4: 'LIGHTING' not found.\n")
}

# ---- Test 5: Alcohol involved vs not (ALCOHOL_INVOLVED) ----
# our cleaning should yield N/Y/Not Stated; we restrict to N vs Y for a clean 2-group test
if ("ALCOHOL_INVOLVED" %in% names(switrs_reduced)) {

  df_alc <- switrs_reduced %>%
    dplyr::filter(!is.na(SevereBinary)) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      ALCOHOL_INVOLVED = trimws(as.character(ALCOHOL_INVOLVED)),
      alc_group = dplyr::case_when(
        ALCOHOL_INVOLVED == "Y" ~ "Alcohol",
        ALCOHOL_INVOLVED == "N" ~ "No Alcohol",
        TRUE ~ NA_character_
      )
    )

  two_prop_report(
    data = df_alc,
    group_var = "alc_group",
    group_a = "Alcohol",
    group_b = "No Alcohol",
    title = "Alcohol involved vs Not alcohol involved"
  )

} else {
  cat("\nSkipping Test 5: 'ALCOHOL_INVOLVED' not found.\n")
}

# ---- Test 6: Rain × Unsafe Speed proxy (interaction) vs not ----
# Uses engineered factor rain_speed = Yes/No if our time-feature chunk ran.
if ("rain_speed" %in% names(switrs_reduced)) {

  df_rs <- switrs_reduced %>%
    dplyr::mutate(rain_speed = as.character(rain_speed)) %>%
    dplyr::filter(rain_speed %in% c("Yes","No"))

  two_prop_report(
    data = df_rs,
    group_var = "rain_speed",
    group_a = "Yes",
    group_b = "No",
    title = "Rain × Unsafe speed proxy (rain_speed=Yes) vs Not (rain_speed=No)"
  )

} else {
  cat("\nSkipping Test 6: 'rain_speed' not found (re-run the time-feature chunk if needed).\n")
}
```

# Scenario Ranking (top combinations & rates & counts)
```{r}
# What: Build interpretable “scenario” groups from time + environment + impairment + pedestrian context,
#       then rank by severe/fatal RATE (risk intensity) and COUNT (severe/fatal burden).
# Why:  This directly answers the client’s decision question: “When and under what conditions should we focus
#       to save the most lives?” Rates identify high-risk contexts; counts identify where impact is largest.

# ---- 1) Build scenario fields (robust to missing columns) ----
df_scen <- switrs_reduced %>%
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(SevereBinary)) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    # TIME features (already derived earlier)
    time_bucket = if ("time_bucket" %in% names(.)) as.character(time_bucket) else NA_character_,
    weekend     = if ("weekend" %in% names(.)) as.character(weekend) else NA_character_,

    # LIGHT: clean contrast Daylight vs Dark (exclude dusk/dawn + not stated for interpretability)
    light = if ("LIGHTING" %in% names(.)) {
      x <- trimws(as.character(LIGHTING))
      dplyr::case_when(
        x == "A" ~ "Daylight",
        x %in% c("C","D","E") ~ "Dark",
        TRUE ~ NA_character_
      )
    } else NA_character_,

    # PEDESTRIAN: use engineered is_ped if present; else fall back to PEDESTRIAN_ACCIDENT
    ped = if ("is_ped" %in% names(.)) {
      x <- as.character(is_ped)  # engineered factor: No/Yes
      dplyr::case_when(
        x %in% c("Yes","No") ~ x,
        TRUE ~ NA_character_
      )
    } else if ("PEDESTRIAN_ACCIDENT" %in% names(.)) {
      x <- trimws(as.character(PEDESTRIAN_ACCIDENT))
      dplyr::case_when(
        x == "Y" ~ "Yes",
        x == "N" ~ "No",
        TRUE ~ NA_character_
      )
    } else NA_character_,

    # IMPAIRMENT SIGNAL: combine crash-level alcohol + PCF DUI proxy (keeps scenario interpretable)
    # What: Collapse to a single Yes/No indicator.
    # Why: Prevents over-fragmenting scenarios while still capturing impairment risk.
    alc_yesno = if ("ALCOHOL_INVOLVED" %in% names(.)) {
      x <- trimws(as.character(ALCOHOL_INVOLVED))  # N/Y/Not Stated
      dplyr::case_when(
        x == "Y" ~ "Yes",
        x == "N" ~ "No",
        TRUE ~ NA_character_
      )
    } else NA_character_,

    dui_yesno = if ("pcf_dui" %in% names(.)) {
      x <- as.character(pcf_dui) # engineered factor: No/Yes
      dplyr::case_when(
        x %in% c("Yes","No") ~ x,
        TRUE ~ NA_character_
      )
    } else NA_character_,

    impairment = dplyr::case_when(
      alc_yesno == "Yes" | dui_yesno == "Yes" ~ "Yes",
      alc_yesno == "No"  & dui_yesno == "No"  ~ "No",
      # if one is missing but the other says Yes, we already catch it above
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    ),

    # OPTIONAL modifiers (kept for an add-on table below)
    rain = if ("is_rain" %in% names(.)) {
      x <- as.character(is_rain)
      dplyr::case_when(x %in% c("Yes","No") ~ x, TRUE ~ NA_character_)
    } else NA_character_,

    speed = if ("pcf_speed" %in% names(.)) {
      x <- as.character(pcf_speed)
      dplyr::case_when(x %in% c("Yes","No") ~ x, TRUE ~ NA_character_)
    } else NA_character_
  )

# ---- 2) Core scenario dimensions (kept intentionally small for stable denominators) ----
scenario_vars <- c("time_bucket","weekend","light","impairment","ped")
scenario_vars <- scenario_vars[scenario_vars %in% names(df_scen)]

df_scen2 <- df_scen %>%
  dplyr::filter(dplyr::if_all(dplyr::all_of(scenario_vars), ~ !is.na(.x)))

cat("Rows with complete CORE scenario fields:", nrow(df_scen2), "\n")
cat("Scenario dimensions used:", paste(scenario_vars, collapse = ", "), "\n")

# ---- 3) Create scenario label and rank ----
df_rank <- df_scen2 %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    scenario = paste(
      paste0("Time=", time_bucket),
      paste0("DayType=", weekend),
      paste0("Light=", light),
      paste0("Impairment=", impairment),
      paste0("Ped=", ped),
      sep = " | "
    ),
    is_severe = (SevereBinary == "Severe/Fatal")
  ) %>%
  dplyr::group_by(scenario) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(
    n_crashes = dplyr::n(),
    severe_n = sum(is_severe, na.rm = TRUE),
    severe_per_100 = 100 * severe_n / n_crashes,
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# ---- 4) Two views: by count and by rate (thresholds tuned for stability) ----
# By COUNT: identifies largest severe/fatal burden scenarios (deployment impact)
min_crashes_count <- 30

top_by_count <- df_rank %>%
  dplyr::filter(n_crashes >= min_crashes_count) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(dplyr::desc(severe_n), dplyr::desc(n_crashes))

cat("\nTop 15 scenarios by Severe/Fatal COUNT",
    "(min_crashes=", min_crashes_count, "):\n", sep = "")
print(head(top_by_count, 15))
cat("\nScenarios meeting COUNT filter:", nrow(top_by_count), "\n")

# By RATE: identifies highest-risk intensity scenarios (targeted intervention)
min_crashes_rate <- 15
min_severe_rate  <- 1

top_by_rate <- df_rank %>%
  dplyr::filter(n_crashes >= min_crashes_rate, severe_n >= min_severe_rate) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(dplyr::desc(severe_per_100), dplyr::desc(n_crashes))

cat("\nTop 15 scenarios by Severe/Fatal RATE",
    "(min_crashes=", min_crashes_rate, ", min_severe=", min_severe_rate, "):\n", sep = "")
print(head(top_by_rate, 15))
cat("\nScenarios meeting RATE filter:", nrow(top_by_rate), "\n")

# ---- 5) Add-on: Interaction flags (quick operational signal checks) ----
# What: Summarize Severe/Fatal rate for engineered interaction terms (Yes vs No).
# Why: These directly operationalize “compound risk conditions” (Night×DUI, Rain×Speeding, Ped×Dark).
interaction_vars <- intersect(c("night_dui","rain_speed","ped_dark"), names(switrs_reduced))

if (length(interaction_vars) > 0) {
  cat("\nInteraction-term summaries (Severe/Fatal per 100 crashes):\n")

  interaction_tbl <- purrr::map_dfr(interaction_vars, function(v) {
    switrs_reduced %>%
      dplyr::filter(!is.na(SevereBinary)) %>%
      dplyr::mutate(g = as.character(.data[[v]])) %>%
      dplyr::filter(g %in% c("Yes","No")) %>%
      dplyr::group_by(term = v, group = g) %>%
      dplyr::summarise(
        n_crashes = dplyr::n(),
        severe_n = sum(SevereBinary == "Severe/Fatal"),
        severe_per_100 = 100 * severe_n / n_crashes,
        .groups = "drop"
      )
  })

  print(interaction_tbl %>% arrange(term, desc(group)))
} else {
  cat("\nNo interaction variables found (night_dui/rain_speed/ped_dark). If we added the time-feature chunk, re-run it.\n")
}
```

# Storyboard panels (FIXED)
```{r storyboard_panels, fig.width=14, fig.height=10}
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(stringr)

base_theme_fix <- theme(
  plot.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold"),
  plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 11),
  plot.margin = margin(t = 12, r = 12, b = 12, l = 12),
  panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
)

# ---- Panel 1: Top Hotspots by Severe/Fatal COUNT ----
if (exists("hotspots_by_count") && nrow(hotspots_by_count) > 0) {

  p1 <- hotspots_by_count %>%
    slice_head(n = 15) %>%
    mutate(location_key = factor(location_key, levels = rev(location_key))) %>%
    ggplot(aes(x = location_key, y = severe_n)) +
    geom_col() +
    coord_flip(clip = "off") +
    labs(
      title = "Top 15 Hotspots by Severe/Fatal Count",
      subtitle = "Higher count = larger severe/fatal burden (deployment prioritization)",
      x = NULL,
      y = "Severe/Fatal crashes"
    ) +
    base_theme_fix

  print(p1)

} else {
  cat("hotspots_by_count not found or empty — run the hotspots chunk first.\n")
}

# ---- Panel 2: Top Scenarios by Severe/Fatal RATE ----
if (exists("top_by_rate") && nrow(top_by_rate) > 0) {

  p2 <- top_by_rate %>%
    slice_head(n = 15) %>%
    mutate(scenario = factor(scenario, levels = rev(scenario))) %>%
    ggplot(aes(x = scenario, y = severe_per_100)) +
    geom_col() +
    coord_flip(clip = "off") +
    labs(
      title = "Top 15 Scenarios by Severe/Fatal Rate",
      subtitle = "Higher rate = higher risk intensity (targeted interventions)",
      x = NULL,
      y = "Severe/Fatal per 100 crashes"
    ) +
    base_theme_fix +
    theme(axis.text.y = element_text(size = 9))

  print(p2)

} else {
  cat("top_by_rate not found or empty — run the scenario ranking chunk first.\n")
}

# ---- Panel 3: Interaction Terms (Yes vs No) ----
# What: Compare Severe/Fatal rates for engineered interaction conditions.
# Why: These are “compound risk” signals (e.g., Night×DUI) that support targeted action.
interaction_vars <- intersect(c("night_dui","rain_speed","ped_dark"), names(switrs_reduced))

if (length(interaction_vars) > 0) {

  interaction_tbl <- purrr::map_dfr(interaction_vars, function(v) {
    switrs_reduced %>%
      filter(!is.na(SevereBinary)) %>%
      mutate(group = as.character(.data[[v]])) %>%
      filter(group %in% c("Yes","No")) %>%
      group_by(term = v, group) %>%
      summarise(
        n_crashes = n(),
        severe_n = sum(SevereBinary == "Severe/Fatal"),
        severe_per_100 = 100 * severe_n / n_crashes,
        .groups = "drop"
      )
  }) %>%
    mutate(
      term_label = recode(
        term,
        night_dui  = "Night × DUI proxy",
        rain_speed = "Rain × Unsafe speed proxy",
        ped_dark   = "Pedestrian × Dark"
      ),
      term_label = factor(term_label, levels = rev(unique(term_label))),
      group = factor(group, levels = c("No","Yes"))
    )

  p3 <- interaction_tbl %>%
    ggplot(aes(x = term_label, y = severe_per_100, fill = group)) +
    geom_col(position = position_dodge(width = 0.8)) +
    coord_flip(clip = "off") +
    labs(
      title = "Compound Risk Conditions (Interaction Terms)",
      subtitle = "Severe/Fatal rate per 100 crashes: compare “Yes” vs “No” for each condition",
      x = NULL,
      y = "Severe/Fatal per 100 crashes",
      fill = "Condition present?"
    ) +
    base_theme_fix

  print(p3)

} else {
  cat("No interaction variables found (night_dui/rain_speed/ped_dark). Re-run the time-feature chunk if needed.\n")
}
```

# codebook label dictionaries
```{r}
# What: Map SWITRS codes -> human-readable labels (used in plots + contingency tables).
# Why: Lets tables/plots display meanings instead of raw codes.

lookup_meaning <- function(x, legend_vec) {
  # x: raw codes; legend_vec: named vector mapping code -> meaning
  x0 <- normalize_not_stated(x, blank_to_notstated = TRUE)

  # exact match
  out <- unname(legend_vec[x0])

  # if missing, try left-pad to width 2 for numeric-looking codes (e.g., PCF_VIOL_CATEGORY)
  needs_pad <- is.na(out) & grepl("^[0-9]+$", x0)
  x2 <- x0
  x2[needs_pad] <- sprintf("%02d", as.integer(x0[needs_pad]))
  out2 <- unname(legend_vec[x2])

  out[is.na(out)] <- out2[is.na(out)]

  # fallback to original code if still missing
  out[is.na(out) | out == ""] <- x0[is.na(out) | out == ""]
  out
}

legend_maps <- list(
  DAY_OF_WEEK = c(
    "1"="Monday","2"="Tuesday","3"="Wednesday","4"="Thursday",
    "5"="Friday","6"="Saturday","7"="Sunday",
    "Not Stated"="Not Stated"
  ),

  # INCLUDED : LIGHTING
  LIGHTING = c(
    "A"="Daylight",
    "B"="Dusk - Dawn",
    "C"="Dark - Street Lights",
    "D"="Dark - No Street Lights",
    "E"="Dark - Street Lights Not Functioning",
    "Not Stated"="Not Stated"
  ),

  WEATHER_1 = c(
    "A"="Clear","B"="Cloudy","C"="Raining","D"="Snowing","E"="Fog","F"="Other","G"="Wind",
    "Not Stated"="Not Stated"
  ),

  ROAD_SURFACE = c(
    "A"="Dry","B"="Wet","C"="Snowy or Icy","D"="Slippery (Muddy/Oily/etc.)",
    "Not Stated"="Not Stated"
  ),

  INTERSECTION = c(
    "Y"="Yes","N"="No",
    "1"="Yes","0"="No",
    "Not Stated"="Not Stated"
  ),

  PRIMARY_COLL_FACTOR = c(
    "A"="Code Violation",
    "B"="Other Improper Driving",
    "C"="Other Than Driver",
    "D"="Unknown",
    "E"="Fell Asleep",
    "Not Stated"="Not Stated"
  ),

  # INCLUDED : TYPE_OF_COLLISION
  TYPE_OF_COLLISION = c(
    "A"="Head-On",
    "B"="Sideswipe",
    "C"="Rear End",
    "D"="Broadside",
    "E"="Hit Object",
    "F"="Overturned",
    "G"="Vehicle/Pedestrian",
    "H"="Other",
    "Not Stated"="Not Stated"
  ),

  PCF_VIOL_CATEGORY = c(
    "01"="DUI (Alcohol/Drug)",
    "02"="Impeding Traffic",
    "03"="Unsafe Speed",
    "04"="Following Too Closely",
    "05"="Wrong Side of Road",
    "06"="Improper Passing",
    "07"="Unsafe Lane Change",
    "08"="Improper Turning",
    "09"="Automobile Right-of-Way",
    "10"="Pedestrian Right-of-Way",
    "11"="Pedestrian Violation",
    "12"="Traffic Signals & Signs",
    "13"="Hazardous Parking",
    "14"="Lights",
    "15"="Brakes",
    "16"="Other Equipment",
    "17"="Other Hazardous Violation",
    "18"="Other Than Driver (or Pedestrian)",
    "21"="Unsafe Starting/Backing",
    "22"="Other Improper Driving",
    "23"="Ped/Other DUI (Alcohol/Drug)",
    "24"="Fell Asleep",
    "00"="Unknown",
    "Not Stated"="Not Stated"
  ),

  HIT_AND_RUN = c(
    "F"="Felony",
    "M"="Misdemeanor",
    "N"="Not Hit and Run",
    "Not Stated"="Not Stated"
  ),

  # INCLUDED : PEDESTRIAN_ACCIDENT
  PEDESTRIAN_ACCIDENT = c(
    "Y"="Yes","N"="No",
    "1"="Yes","0"="No",
    "Not Stated"="Not Stated"
  ),

  # INCLUDED : MOTORCYCLE_ACCIDENT
  MOTORCYCLE_ACCIDENT = c(
    "Y"="Yes","N"="No",
    "1"="Yes","0"="No",
    "Not Stated"="Not Stated"
  ),

  # INCLUDED : ANY_PARTY_DRUG
  ANY_PARTY_DRUG = c(
    "Y"="Yes","N"="No",
    "1"="Yes","0"="No",
    "Not Stated"="Not Stated"
  )
)
# --- Add/override legends for requested variables (minimal patch) ---

legend_maps$TYPE_OF_COLLISION <- c(
  "A"="Head-On",
  "B"="Sideswipe",
  "C"="Rear End",
  "D"="Broadside",
  "E"="Hit Object",
  "F"="Overturned",
  "G"="Vehicle/Pedestrian",
  "H"="Other",
  "Not Stated"="Not Stated"
)

legend_maps$LIGHTING <- c(
  "A"="Daylight",
  "B"="Dusk - Dawn",
  "C"="Dark - Street Lights",
  "D"="Dark - No Street Lights",
  "E"="Dark - Street Lights Not Functioning",
  "Not Stated"="Not Stated"
)

legend_maps$PEDESTRIAN_ACCIDENT <- c(
  "Y"="Yes","N"="No",
  "Not Stated"="Not Stated"
)

legend_maps$MOTORCYCLE_ACCIDENT <- c(
  "Y"="Yes","N"="No",
  "Not Stated"="Not Stated"
)

# Party aggregate name varies by our merge; support both:
legend_maps$ANY_PARTY_DRUG <- c(
  "1"="Yes","0"="No","Y"="Yes","N"="No",
  "Not Stated"="Not Stated"
)
legend_maps$any_party_drug <- legend_maps$ANY_PARTY_DRUG

print_legend <- function(var_name, dedupe_meaning = TRUE) {
  if (!var_name %in% names(legend_maps)) return(invisible(NULL))
  m <- legend_maps[[var_name]]

  df <- data.frame(
    code = names(m),
    meaning = unname(m),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )

  if (dedupe_meaning) df <- df[!duplicated(df$meaning), ]

  cat("\nLEGEND for", var_name, ":\n")
  print(df, row.names = FALSE)
  invisible(df)
}
```

# EDA: Severity rate table for key categorical variables
```{r}
# What: Compute severe rate per 100 crashes by category for a set of variables.
# Why: Decision-oriented EDA: rates are more comparable than raw counts across uneven category sizes.
#      Includes engineered risk flags + interaction terms to show merged-data value-add.

severity_rate_table <- function(data, var) {
  if (!var %in% names(data)) return(NULL)

  df <- data %>%
    dplyr::filter(!is.na(SevereBinary)) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(v = as.character(.data[[var]])) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(v = ifelse(is.na(v) | v == "", "Not Stated", v))

  # Optional: label coded variables for readability (while keeping original codes)
  if (var %in% names(legend_maps)) {
    map <- legend_maps[[var]]
    df <- df %>% mutate(v_label = dplyr::recode(v, !!!map, .default = v))
    grp <- "v_label"
  } else {
    grp <- "v"
  }

  df %>%
    group_by(.data[[grp]]) %>%
    summarise(
      n = n(),
      severe_n = sum(SevereBinary == "Severe/Fatal"),
      severe_per_100 = 100 * severe_n / n,
      .groups = "drop"
    ) %>%
    rename(category = .data[[grp]]) %>%
    arrange(desc(severe_per_100), desc(n))
}

# Include both raw crash fields and engineered flags/interactions (only kept if present)
eda_vars <- intersect(
  c(
    # time / environment
    "DAY_OF_WEEK","time_bucket","weekend","night",
    "WEATHER_1","is_rain",
    "LIGHTING","is_dark",
    "ROAD_SURFACE",
    
    # roadway / crash type
    "INTERSECTION","STATE_HWY_IND","TYPE_OF_COLLISION","PRIMARY_COLL_FACTOR",
    "PCF_VIOL_CATEGORY","pcf_dui","pcf_speed",
    "HIT_AND_RUN",

    # crash flags
    "ALCOHOL_INVOLVED",
    "PEDESTRIAN_ACCIDENT","BICYCLE_ACCIDENT","MOTORCYCLE_ACCIDENT","TRUCK_ACCIDENT",

    # interaction terms (proposal-aligned)
    "night_dui","rain_speed","ped_dark"
  ),
  names(switrs_reduced)
)

rate_tables <- purrr::map(eda_vars, ~ severity_rate_table(switrs_reduced, .x))
names(rate_tables) <- eda_vars

# Show a quick preview of the first few tables
rate_tables[1:min(6, length(rate_tables))]
```

# EDA: Decision-oriented severity rate plots (ordered, horizontal)
```{r}
# What: Plot Severe/Fatal share by categorical variables as normalized stacked bars (horizontal).
# Why: Client-ready comparisons: proportions highlight risk composition instead of just volume.
#      Includes engineered flags + interaction terms to demonstrate merged-data value-add.

plot_severity_by_variable <- function(var_name,
                                      data = switrs_reduced,
                                      fill_var = "SevereBinary") {

  if (!var_name %in% names(data)) return(NULL)

  # Make legend stable across plots
  data[[fill_var]] <- factor(data[[fill_var]], levels = c("Other", "Severe/Fatal"))

  # Build summarized frame (codes preserved in x_code)
  df <- data %>%
    dplyr::filter(!is.na(.data[[fill_var]])) %>%
    dplyr::transmute(
      x_code = normalize_not_stated(.data[[var_name]]),
      Outcome = .data[[fill_var]]
    ) %>%
    dplyr::count(x_code, Outcome, name = "n") %>%
    dplyr::group_by(x_code) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      prop = n / sum(n),
      pct_label = scales::percent(prop, accuracy = 0.1)
    ) %>%
    dplyr::ungroup()

  # Order bars by Severe/Fatal share (descending), tie-break by n
  ordering <- df %>%
    dplyr::group_by(x_code) %>%
    dplyr::summarise(
      severe_prop = sum(prop[Outcome == "Severe/Fatal"], na.rm = TRUE),
      total_n = sum(n),
      .groups = "drop"
    ) %>%
    dplyr::arrange(dplyr::desc(severe_prop), dplyr::desc(total_n)) %>%
    dplyr::pull(x_code)

  df$x_code <- factor(df$x_code, levels = ordering)

  # Axis label function: if a legend exists, show meaning; else show the code
  label_fun <- function(x) {
    if (exists("legend_maps") && var_name %in% names(legend_maps)) {
      out <- lookup_meaning(x, legend_maps[[var_name]])
      out[is.na(out) | out == ""] <- x[is.na(out) | out == ""]
      out
    } else {
      x
    }
  }

  p <- ggplot(df, aes(x = x_code, y = prop, fill = Outcome)) +
    geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "stack") +
    geom_text(
      aes(label = pct_label),
      position = position_stack(vjust = 0.5),
      color = "white",
      size = 3.6,
      fontface = "bold"
    ) +
    coord_flip() +
    scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0, 0), breaks = NULL, labels = NULL) +
    scale_x_discrete(labels = label_fun) +
    scale_fill_discrete(drop = FALSE) +
    labs(
      title = paste("Severity Distribution by", gsub("_", " ", var_name)),
      x = gsub("_", " ", var_name),
      y = NULL,
      fill = "Outcome"
    ) +
    theme(
      axis.text.x = element_blank(),
      axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
      panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
      panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank(),
      legend.position = "right"
    )

  print(p)

  # Print legend mapping only for coded variables
  if (exists("print_legend") && exists("legend_maps") && var_name %in% names(legend_maps)) {
    print_legend(var_name)
  }
}

# Choose a manageable set to render (includes engineered flags + interactions if present)
vars_to_plot <- intersect(
  c(
    # when
    "time_bucket","weekend","night",

    # environment
    "WEATHER_1","is_rain",
    "LIGHTING","is_dark",
    "ROAD_SURFACE",

    # roadway / crash type
    "INTERSECTION","STATE_HWY_IND","TYPE_OF_COLLISION","PRIMARY_COLL_FACTOR",

    # violations / risk proxies
    "PCF_VIOL_CATEGORY","pcf_dui","pcf_speed",

    # proposal-aligned interactions (high-value)
    "night_dui","rain_speed","ped_dark"
  ),
  names(switrs_reduced)
)

purrr::walk(vars_to_plot, ~ plot_severity_by_variable(.x))
```

# Correlation Check (numeric predictors)
```{r}
# What: Correlation scan among numeric predictors.
# Why: Highly collinear predictors can destabilize regression coefficients and interpretation.

num_vars <- names(switrs_reduced)[sapply(switrs_reduced, is.numeric)]
num_vars <- setdiff(num_vars, c("ACCIDENT_YEAR"))  # often redundant with derived year

if (length(num_vars) >= 2) {
  cor_matrix <- cor(switrs_reduced[num_vars], use = "pairwise.complete.obs")
  corrplot(cor_matrix, method = "color", type = "lower", tl.cex = 0.6)

  # Report high/moderate correlations
  high_corr <- c()
  moderate_corr <- c()

  for (i in 1:(ncol(cor_matrix) - 1)) {
    for (j in (i + 1):ncol(cor_matrix)) {
      r <- cor_matrix[i, j]
      if (!is.na(r)) {
        pair <- paste(colnames(cor_matrix)[i], colnames(cor_matrix)[j], "r =", round(r, 3))
        if (abs(r) >= 0.8) high_corr <- c(high_corr, pair)
        else if (abs(r) >= 0.5) moderate_corr <- c(moderate_corr, pair)
      }
    }
  }

  cat("High correlations (|r| >= 0.8):\n")
  high_corr
  cat("\nModerate correlations (0.5 <= |r| < 0.8):\n")
  moderate_corr
} else {
  cat("Not enough numeric columns for correlation analysis.\n")
}
```

# Baseline modeling dataset
```{r}
# BASELINE MODEL (CLEAN PIPELINE): build model_df safely, drop separators, train/test eval
# What: Construct modeling frame (no leakage), handle missingness consistently, fit GLM, and report AUC + ORs.
# Why: Provides an interpretable baseline model and validates which conditions increase severe/fatal risk.

set.seed(544)

# ---- 1) Candidate predictors (includes engineered flags + interactions if present) ----
candidate_predictors <- intersect(
  c(
    # crash-level
    "year","time_bucket","DAY_OF_WEEK","weekend",
    "WEATHER_1","LIGHTING","ROAD_SURFACE",
    "INTERSECTION","STATE_HWY_IND","TYPE_OF_COLLISION","PRIMARY_COLL_FACTOR",
    "PCF_VIOL_CATEGORY","HIT_AND_RUN","ALCOHOL_INVOLVED",
    "PEDESTRIAN_ACCIDENT","BICYCLE_ACCIDENT","MOTORCYCLE_ACCIDENT","TRUCK_ACCIDENT",

    # engineered indicators + interactions
    "night","pcf_dui","pcf_speed","is_rain","is_dark","is_ped",
    "night_dui","rain_speed","ped_dark",

    # party aggregates (if present in merged file)
    "n_parties","any_party_alcohol","any_party_drug","any_party_at_fault","mean_party_age",

    # victim aggregates (non-leakage only)
    "n_victims","min_victim_age","mean_victim_age","max_victim_age"
  ),
  names(switrs_reduced)
)

# Remove any leakage vars if they were accidentally included
candidate_predictors <- setdiff(candidate_predictors, leakage_vars)

# Hard stop if anything banned still sneaks in
if (length(intersect(candidate_predictors, leakage_vars)) > 0) {
  stop("Leakage detected: a harm/outcome variable is in candidate_predictors.")
}

# ---- 2) Build modeling frame (NO phat in training data) ----
model_df_clean <- switrs_reduced %>%
  dplyr::select(all_of(c("SevereBinary", candidate_predictors))) %>%
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(SevereBinary)) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(SevereBinary = factor(SevereBinary, levels = c("Other","Severe/Fatal")))

# Types: chars->factor; explicit NA label for factors
model_df_clean <- model_df_clean %>%
  dplyr::mutate(across(where(is.character), as.factor)) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(across(where(is.factor), ~ forcats::fct_explicit_na(.x, na_level = "Not Stated")))

# Numeric: median impute + missing indicator
num_cols <- names(model_df_clean)[sapply(model_df_clean, is.numeric)]
for (v in num_cols) {
  miss_name <- paste0(v, "_miss")
  model_df_clean[[miss_name]] <- as.integer(is.na(model_df_clean[[v]]))
  med <- suppressWarnings(median(model_df_clean[[v]], na.rm = TRUE))
  if (is.na(med)) med <- 0
  model_df_clean[[v]][is.na(model_df_clean[[v]])] <- med
}

# Drop constant predictors
is_constant <- sapply(model_df_clean %>% dplyr::select(-SevereBinary), function(x) {
  ux <- unique(x); ux <- ux[!is.na(ux)]
  length(ux) <= 1
})
if (any(is_constant)) {
  cat("Dropping constant predictors:\n")
  print(names(is_constant)[is_constant])
  model_df_clean <- model_df_clean %>% dplyr::select(SevereBinary, names(is_constant)[!is_constant])
}

# ---- 3) Auto-detect *practical* separation and drop those predictors ----
# What: Flag predictors that cause quasi/perfect separation with meaningful support.
# Why: Prevents non-convergence / infinite estimates in unpenalized logistic regression.
y <- model_df_clean$SevereBinary
pred_names <- setdiff(names(model_df_clean), "SevereBinary")

is_perfect_separator <- function(x, y, min_level_n = 30) {
  if (is.character(x) || is.factor(x)) {
    tab <- table(x, y, useNA = "no")
    # only consider levels with enough support
    lvl_n <- rowSums(tab)
    tab2 <- tab[lvl_n >= min_level_n, , drop = FALSE]
    if (nrow(tab2) == 0) return(FALSE)

    # separation if any retained level has 0 in one outcome class
    any(apply(tab2, 1, function(r) any(r == 0) && sum(r) > 0))
  } else if (is.numeric(x) || is.integer(x)) {
    x1 <- x[y == "Severe/Fatal"]; x0 <- x[y == "Other"]
    x1 <- x1[is.finite(x1)]; x0 <- x0[is.finite(x0)]
    if (length(x1) < 30 || length(x0) < 30) return(FALSE)
    (max(x0, na.rm = TRUE) < min(x1, na.rm = TRUE)) || (max(x1, na.rm = TRUE) < min(x0, na.rm = TRUE))
  } else FALSE
}

sep_flags <- sapply(pred_names, function(v) is_perfect_separator(model_df_clean[[v]], y))
bad_vars <- names(sep_flags)[sep_flags]

cat("Predictors dropped for practical separation:", length(bad_vars), "\n")
if (length(bad_vars) > 0) print(bad_vars)

model_df_clean2 <- model_df_clean %>% dplyr::select(-dplyr::all_of(bad_vars))

cat("Final predictors used:", ncol(model_df_clean2) - 1, "\n")
cat("Rows used:", nrow(model_df_clean2), "\n")

# ---- 4) Train/Test split (stratified) ----
idx_sev <- which(model_df_clean2$SevereBinary == "Severe/Fatal")
idx_oth <- which(model_df_clean2$SevereBinary == "Other")
test_frac <- 0.30

test_idx <- c(
  sample(idx_sev, size = floor(length(idx_sev) * test_frac)),
  sample(idx_oth, size = floor(length(idx_oth) * test_frac))
)

test_set  <- model_df_clean2[test_idx, , drop = FALSE]
train_set <- model_df_clean2[-test_idx, , drop = FALSE]

cat("Train n =", nrow(train_set), " | Test n =", nrow(test_set), "\n")

# ---- 5) Fit GLM (train-only) ----
glm_fit_final <- glm(
  SevereBinary ~ .,
  data = train_set,
  family = binomial(),
  control = glm.control(maxit = 50)
)
glm_fit_final

cat("GLM converged? ", glm_fit_final$converged, "\n", sep = "")

# ---- 6) Predict + AUC + confusion matrix ----
test_phat <- as.numeric(predict(glm_fit_final, newdata = test_set, type = "response"))
test_y <- test_set$SevereBinary

if (!requireNamespace("pROC", quietly = TRUE)) install.packages("pROC")
if (requireNamespace("pROC", quietly = TRUE)) {
  roc_obj <- pROC::roc(test_y, test_phat, levels = c("Other","Severe/Fatal"), quiet = TRUE)
  cat("TEST AUC:", round(as.numeric(pROC::auc(roc_obj)), 4), "\n")
}

pred_05 <- factor(ifelse(test_phat >= 0.5, "Severe/Fatal", "Other"), levels = levels(test_y))
cat("\nConfusion matrix (cutoff=0.50):\n")
print(table(Pred = pred_05, Truth = test_y))

# Prevalence cutoff (often more useful under imbalance)
prev <- mean(train_set$SevereBinary == "Severe/Fatal")
pred_prev <- factor(ifelse(test_phat >= prev, "Severe/Fatal", "Other"), levels = levels(test_y))
cat("\nConfusion matrix (cutoff=prevalence=", round(prev, 4), "):\n", sep = "")
print(table(Pred = pred_prev, Truth = test_y))

# ---- 7) Interpretation aid: Odds ratios (FAST Wald CI) ----
# What: Compute odds ratios + 95% Wald CIs from GLM coefficients.
# Why: broom::tidy(..., conf.int=TRUE) can be very slow for glm because it may profile CIs.

coefs <- summary(glm_fit_final)$coefficients
or_tbl <- data.frame(
  term = rownames(coefs),
  estimate = coefs[, "Estimate"],
  std.error = coefs[, "Std. Error"],
  p.value = coefs[, "Pr(>|z|)"],
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
) %>%
  dplyr::filter(term != "(Intercept)") %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    OR = exp(estimate),
    conf.low = exp(estimate - 1.96 * std.error),
    conf.high = exp(estimate + 1.96 * std.error),
    log_or = abs(estimate)
  ) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(dplyr::desc(log_or)) %>%
  dplyr::select(term, OR, conf.low, conf.high, p.value)

cat("\nTop 20 GLM odds ratios by |log(OR)| (FAST Wald CI, train fit):\n")
print(head(or_tbl, 20))
```

# What-if / one-at-a-time probability changes (GLM)
```{r}
# What: Build a "typical crash" baseline row, then vary one predictor while holding others fixed.
# Why: Turns the model into an actionable simulator: "if we change X, how does risk move?"

if (!exists("glm_fit_final")) stop("glm_fit_final not found. Run the baseline GLM chunk first.")
if (!exists("train_set")) stop("train_set not found. Run the baseline GLM chunk first.")

# --- Reference predictor frame (exact columns & factor levels used in the GLM) ---
model_vars <- setdiff(names(train_set), "SevereBinary")
X_ref <- train_set[, model_vars, drop = FALSE]

# --- Helpers to build a baseline profile ---
mode_level <- function(x) {
  x <- x[!is.na(x)]
  if (length(x) == 0) return(NA)
  names(which.max(table(x)))[1]
}

baseline_row <- as.data.frame(lapply(X_ref, function(col) {
  if (is.numeric(col) || is.integer(col)) {
    as.numeric(stats::median(col, na.rm = TRUE))
  } else {
    mode_level(as.character(col))
  }
}), stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

# restore factor types/levels exactly
for (v in names(X_ref)) {
  if (is.factor(X_ref[[v]])) {
    baseline_row[[v]] <- factor(baseline_row[[v]], levels = levels(X_ref[[v]]))
  } else if (is.integer(X_ref[[v]])) {
    baseline_row[[v]] <- as.integer(round(as.numeric(baseline_row[[v]])))
  } else if (is.numeric(X_ref[[v]])) {
    baseline_row[[v]] <- as.numeric(baseline_row[[v]])
  }
}

# baseline probability
p0 <- as.numeric(predict(glm_fit_final, newdata = baseline_row, type = "response"))

# --- Main function: vary one variable, hold the rest fixed ---
what_if <- function(var_name, values = NULL, model = glm_fit_final, base = baseline_row, ref = X_ref) {
  if (!var_name %in% names(base)) stop("Variable not in model predictors: ", var_name)

  xref <- ref[[var_name]]

  # default values if none supplied
  if (is.null(values)) {
    if (is.factor(xref) || is.character(xref)) {
      values <- if (is.factor(xref)) levels(xref) else sort(unique(na.omit(xref)))
    } else {
      # numeric: show 10th/50th/90th percentiles
      qs <- stats::quantile(xref, probs = c(0.10, 0.50, 0.90), na.rm = TRUE, names = FALSE)
      values <- as.numeric(qs)
    }
  }

  # baseline value (for delta)
  base_val <- as.character(base[[var_name]])

  out <- lapply(values, function(val) {
    nd <- base

    if (is.factor(xref)) {
      nd[[var_name]] <- factor(as.character(val), levels = levels(xref))
    } else if (is.integer(xref)) {
      nd[[var_name]] <- as.integer(val)
    } else if (is.numeric(xref)) {
      nd[[var_name]] <- as.numeric(val)
    } else {
      nd[[var_name]] <- as.character(val)
    }

    p <- as.numeric(predict(model, newdata = nd, type = "response"))
    data.frame(
      variable = var_name,
      value = as.character(val),
      p_severe = p,
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    )
  })

  out <- dplyr::bind_rows(out) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      p_baseline = p0,
      delta_pp = 100 * (p_severe - p_baseline),
      baseline_value = base_val
    ) %>%
    dplyr::arrange(dplyr::desc(p_severe))

  out
}

# ---- Example 1 (recommended): weekend what-if ----
if ("weekend" %in% names(baseline_row)) {
  wk_vals <- intersect(c("Weekday","Weekend"), levels(X_ref$weekend))
  what_if_weekend <- what_if("weekend", values = wk_vals)
  print(what_if_weekend)
} else {
  cat("weekend not in model predictors.\n")
}

what_if("LIGHTING", c("A","C","D","E","Not Stated"))
what_if("pcf_speed", c("No","Yes"))
what_if("WEATHER_1")
what_if("PCF_VIOL_CATEGORY")
what_if("time_bucket")
```

# Penalized logistic regression: GLMNET model
```{r glmnet_model, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# MODEL UPGRADE: Penalized Logistic Regression (glmnet)
# What: Fit LASSO logistic regression with cross-validation using a consistent one-hot encoding
#       across train and test sets.
# Why:  model.matrix() must produce the same columns for train/test; building them separately can
#       create mismatches when factor levels differ by split. This version guarantees alignment.

# Install/load glmnet if needed
if (!requireNamespace("glmnet", quietly = TRUE)) install.packages("glmnet")
library(glmnet)

# ---- 1) Sanity checks ----
if (!exists("train_set") || !exists("test_set")) {
  stop("train_set/test_set not found. Run the baseline modeling chunk first.")
}
if (!("SevereBinary" %in% names(train_set))) stop("SevereBinary not found in train_set.")

# ---- 2) Build aligned model matrices (one-hot encode factors consistently) ----
# Add split flag, bind, encode once, then split back
train_set$.split <- "train"
test_set$.split  <- "test"
combo <- dplyr::bind_rows(train_set, test_set)

# model.matrix will create consistent columns because it's run once on the combined data
X_all <- model.matrix(SevereBinary ~ . , data = combo)[, -1, drop = FALSE]

# Split rows back
train_idx <- combo$.split == "train"
test_idx  <- combo$.split == "test"

x_train <- X_all[train_idx, , drop = FALSE]
x_test  <- X_all[test_idx,  , drop = FALSE]

# Outcome as 0/1: Severe/Fatal = 1
y_train <- as.integer(combo$SevereBinary[train_idx] == "Severe/Fatal")
y_test  <- as.integer(combo$SevereBinary[test_idx]  == "Severe/Fatal")

# Cleanup temp columns
train_set$.split <- NULL
test_set$.split <- NULL
combo$.split <- NULL

# ---- 3) Cross-validated glmnet ----
# alpha = 1 (LASSO). ridge: 0. elastic net: between 0 and 1.
set.seed(544)
cv_fit <- cv.glmnet(
  x = x_train,
  y = y_train,
  family = "binomial",
  alpha = 1,
  nfolds = 5,
  type.measure = "auc"
)

cat("Best lambda (min):", cv_fit$lambda.min, "\n")
cat("1-SE lambda:", cv_fit$lambda.1se, "\n")

plot(cv_fit)

# ---- 4) Predict on test + AUC ----
p_test <- as.numeric(predict(cv_fit, newx = x_test, s = "lambda.1se", type = "response"))

if (!requireNamespace("pROC", quietly = TRUE)) install.packages("pROC")
auc_glmnet <- NA_real_
if (requireNamespace("pROC", quietly = TRUE)) {
  roc_obj <- pROC::roc(response = y_test, predictor = p_test, quiet = TRUE)
  auc_glmnet <- as.numeric(pROC::auc(roc_obj))
  cat("TEST AUC (glmnet, lambda.1se):", round(auc_glmnet, 4), "\n")
}

# Confusion matrices at 0.50 and prevalence cutoff
pred_05 <- ifelse(p_test >= 0.5, 1, 0)
cat("\nConfusion matrix (glmnet, cutoff=0.50):\n")
print(table(Pred = pred_05, Truth = y_test))
```
