```{r glmnet_model, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
prev <- mean(y_train == 1)
pred_prev <- ifelse(p_test >= prev, 1, 0)
cat("\nConfusion matrix (glmnet, cutoff=prevalence=", round(prev, 4), "):\n", sep = "")
print(table(Pred = pred_prev, Truth = y_test))

# ---- 5) Top coefficients (interpretation aid) ----
coef_mat <- as.matrix(coef(cv_fit, s = "lambda.1se"))
coef_df <- data.frame(
  term = rownames(coef_mat),
  coef = as.numeric(coef_mat[, 1]),
  row.names = NULL
) %>%
  dplyr::filter(term != "(Intercept)", coef != 0) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(dplyr::desc(abs(coef)))

cat("\nTop 20 non-zero coefficients by |coef| (glmnet, lambda.1se):\n")
print(head(coef_df, 20))
```

# Feature selection(glmnet LASSO)
```{r feature_selection_summary}
# What: Extract selected predictors from glmnet (non-zero coefficients at lambda.1se).
# Why: Makes “feature selection” explicit + reportable (which variables survived selection).

if (!exists("cv_fit")) stop("cv_fit not found. Run glmnet_model chunk first.")

# Non-zero coefficients at lambda.1se
coef_mat <- as.matrix(coef(cv_fit, s = "lambda.1se"))
coef_df <- data.frame(
  term = rownames(coef_mat),
  coef = as.numeric(coef_mat[, 1]),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
) %>%
  dplyr::filter(term != "(Intercept)", coef != 0) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(dplyr::desc(abs(coef)))

cat("Number of selected (non-zero) one-hot terms:", nrow(coef_df), "\n\n")
cat("Top 30 selected terms by |coef|:\n")
print(head(coef_df, 30))

# Map one-hot terms back to their base variable names (best-effort)
# e.g., WEATHER_1C -> WEATHER_1, time_bucketPM... -> time_bucket
candidate_base_vars <- setdiff(names(train_set), c("SevereBinary"))  # includes *_miss too
candidate_base_vars <- candidate_base_vars[order(nchar(candidate_base_vars), decreasing = TRUE)]

base_var_from_term <- function(term, vars) {
  hits <- vars[startsWith(term, vars)]
  if (length(hits) == 0) return(term)
  hits[1]  # vars sorted longest-first, so first hit is best match
}

coef_df$base_var <- vapply(coef_df$term, base_var_from_term, character(1), vars = candidate_base_vars)

selected_vars <- coef_df %>%
  dplyr::count(base_var, sort = TRUE)

cat("\nSelected base variables (ranked by how many active dummy terms they contribute):\n")
print(selected_vars)
```

# What-if for the most important (LASSO selected) features
```{r}
# (3) WHAT-IF ANALYSIS (FIXED): uses glm_fit_final$xlevels so predict() never sees "new levels"
if (!exists("glm_fit_final")) stop("glm_fit_final not found. Run baseline GLM chunk first.")
if (!exists("train_set")) stop("train_set not found. Run baseline GLM chunk first.")

model_vars <- setdiff(names(train_set), "SevereBinary")
X_ref <- train_set[, model_vars, drop = FALSE]

# Model factor levels (source of truth for predict())
model_xlev <- glm_fit_final$xlevels  # named list for factor predictors

mode_level <- function(x) {
  x <- x[!is.na(x)]
  if (length(x) == 0) return(NA_character_)
  names(which.max(table(x)))[1]
}

safe_predict_prob <- function(model, newdata) {
  # suppresses rank-deficient warning spam; prediction still computed
  as.numeric(suppressWarnings(predict(model, newdata = newdata, type = "response")))
}

# ---- Build a "typical crash" baseline row that is guaranteed valid for the model ----
baseline_row <- as.data.frame(lapply(names(X_ref), function(v) {
  col <- X_ref[[v]]

  # factor predictors: pick most common observed value that is allowed by model_xlev
  if (v %in% names(model_xlev)) {
    allowed <- model_xlev[[v]]
    obs_mode <- mode_level(as.character(col))
    # if mode not allowed (or NA), fall back to most frequent allowed level
    if (is.na(obs_mode) || !(obs_mode %in% allowed)) {
      freq <- sort(table(as.character(col)), decreasing = TRUE)
      cand <- intersect(names(freq), allowed)
      obs_mode <- if (length(cand) > 0) cand[1] else allowed[1]
    }
    return(factor(obs_mode, levels = allowed))
  }

  # numeric/integer predictors
  if (is.numeric(col) || is.integer(col)) {
    return(as.numeric(stats::median(col, na.rm = TRUE)))
  }

  # character predictors (rare here if model was built correctly)
  mode_level(as.character(col))
}), stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

names(baseline_row) <- names(X_ref)

# Restore integer type where appropriate
for (v in names(X_ref)) {
  if (is.integer(X_ref[[v]]) && !(v %in% names(model_xlev))) {
    baseline_row[[v]] <- as.integer(round(as.numeric(baseline_row[[v]])))
  }
}

p0 <- safe_predict_prob(glm_fit_final, baseline_row)

# ---- Main what-if function (guarantees factor levels match model_xlev) ----
what_if <- function(var_name, values = NULL,
                    model = glm_fit_final, base = baseline_row, ref = X_ref, xlev = model_xlev) {

  if (!var_name %in% names(base)) stop("Variable not in model predictors: ", var_name)

  # Factor predictor in the model
  if (var_name %in% names(xlev)) {
    allowed <- xlev[[var_name]]

    if (is.null(values)) values <- allowed
    values <- as.character(values)
    values <- values[values %in% allowed]

    if (length(values) == 0) stop("No provided values are valid levels for ", var_name)

    out <- lapply(values, function(val) {
      nd <- base
      nd[[var_name]] <- factor(val, levels = allowed)
      p <- safe_predict_prob(model, nd)
      data.frame(variable = var_name, value = val, p_severe = p, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
    })

    out <- dplyr::bind_rows(out) %>%
      dplyr::mutate(
        p_baseline = p0,
        delta_pp = 100 * (p_severe - p_baseline),
        baseline_value = as.character(base[[var_name]])
      ) %>%
      dplyr::arrange(dplyr::desc(p_severe))

    return(out)
  }

  # Numeric predictor
  xref <- ref[[var_name]]
  if (is.null(values)) {
    qs <- stats::quantile(xref, probs = c(0.10, 0.50, 0.90), na.rm = TRUE, names = FALSE)
    values <- as.numeric(qs)
  }

  out <- lapply(values, function(val) {
    nd <- base
    if (is.integer(xref)) nd[[var_name]] <- as.integer(val) else nd[[var_name]] <- as.numeric(val)
    p <- safe_predict_prob(model, nd)
    data.frame(variable = var_name, value = as.character(val), p_severe = p, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  })

  dplyr::bind_rows(out) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      p_baseline = p0,
      delta_pp = 100 * (p_severe - p_baseline),
      baseline_value = as.character(base[[var_name]])
    ) %>%
    dplyr::arrange(dplyr::desc(p_severe))
}
# (3) WHAT-IF FOR MOST IMPORTANT FEATURES (LASSO base vars) — FIXED
if (!exists("selected_vars")) stop("selected_vars not found. Run feature_selection_summary first.")

top_k <- 10

important_vars <- selected_vars %>%
  dplyr::arrange(dplyr::desc(n)) %>%
  dplyr::pull(base_var) %>%
  unique()

important_vars <- important_vars[!grepl("_miss$", important_vars)]
important_vars <- important_vars[important_vars %in% names(baseline_row)]
important_vars <- head(important_vars, top_k)

cat("Most important variables (top ", top_k, "):\n", sep = "")
print(important_vars)

# choose up to K levels for high-cardinality factors, always including baseline value
pick_levels <- function(var_name, ref, xlev, max_show = 8) {
  allowed <- xlev[[var_name]]
  if (length(allowed) <= max_show) return(allowed)

  base_val <- as.character(baseline_row[[var_name]])
  freq <- sort(table(as.character(ref[[var_name]])), decreasing = TRUE)
  top_levs <- intersect(names(freq), allowed)
  vals <- unique(c(base_val, head(top_levs, max_show)))
  vals[vals %in% allowed]
}

whatif_tables <- list()
for (v in important_vars) {
  if (v %in% names(glm_fit_final$xlevels)) {
    vals <- pick_levels(v, train_set, glm_fit_final$xlevels, max_show = 8)
    whatif_tables[[v]] <- what_if(v, values = vals)
  } else {
    whatif_tables[[v]] <- what_if(v)  # numeric -> 10/50/90 by default
  }
}

whatif_summary <- purrr::imap_dfr(whatif_tables, function(tbl, varname) {
  best <- tbl[which.max(tbl$p_severe), , drop = FALSE]
  worst <- tbl[which.min(tbl$p_severe), , drop = FALSE]
  data.frame(
    variable = varname,
    baseline_value = best$baseline_value[1],
    baseline_p = best$p_baseline[1],
    best_value = best$value[1],
    best_p = best$p_severe[1],
    best_delta_pp = best$delta_pp[1],
    worst_value = worst$value[1],
    worst_p = worst$p_severe[1],
    worst_delta_pp = worst$delta_pp[1],
    swing_pp = best$delta_pp[1] - worst$delta_pp[1],
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
}) %>% dplyr::arrange(dplyr::desc(abs(swing_pp)))

cat("\n================ WHAT-IF SUMMARY (top features) ================\n")
print(whatif_summary)
```

# UNIVARIATE HYPOTHESIS TESTING HELPERS (t-test / Wilcoxon / prop-test / chi-square)
```{r}
# What: Provide a single standardized pipeline to test association between each predictor and SevereBinary.
# Why: Matches STAT 544 “statistics report” expectations: t-tests for numeric vars, two-proportion tests for
#      binary predictors, and chi-square independence tests for categorical predictors.

library(dplyr)
library(purrr)
library(broom)
library(tibble)

# --- Effect sizes ---
cohen_d <- function(x, g) {
  # x numeric; g factor with levels c("Other","Severe/Fatal")
  x0 <- x[g == "Other"]; x1 <- x[g == "Severe/Fatal"]
  x0 <- x0[is.finite(x0)]; x1 <- x1[is.finite(x1)]
  if (length(x0) < 2 || length(x1) < 2) return(NA_real_)
  s0 <- sd(x0); s1 <- sd(x1)
  sp <- sqrt(((length(x0)-1)*s0^2 + (length(x1)-1)*s1^2) / (length(x0)+length(x1)-2))
  if (!is.finite(sp) || sp == 0) return(NA_real_)
  (mean(x1) - mean(x0)) / sp
}

cramers_v <- function(tab, chi2_stat) {
  n <- sum(tab)
  r <- nrow(tab); c <- ncol(tab)
  if (n == 0 || min(r-1, c-1) <= 0) return(NA_real_)
  sqrt(as.numeric(chi2_stat) / (n * min(r-1, c-1)))
}

# --- Main per-variable test ---
run_univariate_test <- function(data, var,
                                outcome = "SevereBinary",
                                alpha = 0.05,
                                max_levels = 60,
                                chisq_B = 2000) {
  if (!all(c(var, outcome) %in% names(data))) return(NULL)

  df <- data %>%
    dplyr::select(all_of(c(var, outcome))) %>%
    dplyr::filter(!is.na(.data[[outcome]]))

  # Ensure outcome levels
  df[[outcome]] <- factor(df[[outcome]], levels = c("Other", "Severe/Fatal"))
  df <- df %>% dplyr::filter(!is.na(.data[[outcome]]))

  x <- df[[var]]

  # Treat numeric 0/1 (or 2 unique values) as categorical
  if (is.numeric(x) || is.integer(x)) {
    ux <- unique(x[is.finite(x)])
    if (length(ux) <= 2) x <- as.factor(x)
  }

  # -------------------------
  # NUMERIC predictor: t-test + Wilcoxon
  # -------------------------
  if (is.numeric(x) || is.integer(x)) {
    df2 <- df %>% mutate(x = as.numeric(.data[[var]])) %>% filter(is.finite(x))
    if (nrow(df2) < 10) return(tibble(
      variable = var, type = "numeric", n_used = nrow(df2),
      test = "SKIP (too few non-missing)", statistic = NA_real_,
      p_value = NA_real_, effect = NA_real_, effect_label = NA_character_
    ))

    # Welch t-test (default; robust)
    tt_welch <- tryCatch(t.test(x ~ .data[[outcome]], data = df2, var.equal = FALSE), error = function(e) NULL)
    # Student t-test (equal variance) explicitly shown for the requirement
    tt_student <- tryCatch(t.test(x ~ .data[[outcome]], data = df2, var.equal = TRUE), error = function(e) NULL)

    # Wilcoxon as nonparametric backup for skew / non-normal settings (notes include Wilcoxon) 
    wt <- tryCatch(wilcox.test(x ~ .data[[outcome]], data = df2, exact = FALSE), error = function(e) NULL)

    m_other <- mean(df2$x[df2[[outcome]] == "Other"], na.rm = TRUE)
    m_sev   <- mean(df2$x[df2[[outcome]] == "Severe/Fatal"], na.rm = TRUE)
    d <- cohen_d(df2$x, df2[[outcome]])

    tibble(
      variable = var,
      type = "numeric",
      n_used = nrow(df2),

      # Report BOTH (prof wants t-tests). Use Welch p as primary unless we want to force Student.
      test = "t-test (Welch) + t-test (Student) + Wilcoxon",
      statistic = if (!is.null(tt_welch)) unname(tt_welch$statistic) else NA_real_,
      p_value = if (!is.null(tt_welch)) tt_welch$p.value else NA_real_,

      p_value_student = if (!is.null(tt_student)) tt_student$p.value else NA_real_,
      p_value_wilcox  = if (!is.null(wt)) wt$p.value else NA_real_,

      mean_other = m_other,
      mean_severe = m_sev,
      mean_diff = m_sev - m_other,
      effect = d,
      effect_label = "Cohen_d (Severe - Other)"
    )
  }

  # -------------------------
  # CATEGORICAL predictor: prop-test for 2 levels, chi-square for 2+ levels
  # -------------------------
  x <- as.character(x)
  x[x == ""] <- NA_character_
  df2 <- df %>% mutate(x = x) %>% filter(!is.na(x))

  if (nrow(df2) < 10) return(tibble(
    variable = var, type = "categorical", n_used = nrow(df2),
    test = "SKIP (too few non-missing)", statistic = NA_real_,
    p_value = NA_real_, effect = NA_real_, effect_label = NA_character_
  ))

  # Trim extreme high-cardinality (keeps runtime sane; still “as many as we can”)
  k <- length(unique(df2$x))
  if (k > max_levels) {
    return(tibble(
      variable = var, type = "categorical", n_used = nrow(df2),
      test = paste0("SKIP (high cardinality: ", k, " levels > ", max_levels, ")"),
      statistic = NA_real_, p_value = NA_real_, effect = NA_real_, effect_label = NA_character_
    ))
  }

  tab <- table(df2$x, df2[[outcome]])

  # If exactly 2 levels -> Two-proportion test (matches notes)
  if (nrow(tab) == 2) {
    x_succ <- tab[, "Severe/Fatal"]
    n_tot  <- rowSums(tab)
    pt <- suppressWarnings(prop.test(x = x_succ, n = n_tot, correct = FALSE))

    p1 <- x_succ[1] / n_tot[1]
    p2 <- x_succ[2] / n_tot[2]
    diff <- p1 - p2

    # For effect size: risk difference (p1-p2) and also Cramer's V for 2x2
    v <- cramers_v(tab, pt$statistic)

    tibble(
      variable = var,
      type = "categorical (2 levels)",
      n_used = sum(tab),
      test = "Two-proportion test (prop.test) (≈ Z-test) + 2x2 chi-square equivalent",
      statistic = unname(pt$statistic),
      p_value = pt$p.value,
      level1 = rownames(tab)[1],
      level2 = rownames(tab)[2],
      p_severe_level1 = p1,
      p_severe_level2 = p2,
      risk_diff = diff,
      effect = v,
      effect_label = "CramersV (2x2)"
    )
  } else {
    # 2+ levels -> Chi-square independence test
    cs <- suppressWarnings(chisq.test(tab))
    use_sim <- any(cs$expected < 5)
    cs2 <- if (use_sim) suppressWarnings(chisq.test(tab, simulate.p.value = TRUE, B = chisq_B)) else cs
    v <- cramers_v(tab, cs2$statistic)

    tibble(
      variable = var,
      type = paste0("categorical (", nrow(tab), " levels)"),
      n_used = sum(tab),
      test = if (use_sim) paste0("Chi-square independence (sim p, B=", chisq_B, ")") else "Chi-square independence",
      statistic = unname(cs2$statistic),
      p_value = cs2$p.value,
      df = unname(cs2$parameter),
      effect = v,
      effect_label = "CramersV"
    )
  }
}

run_univariate_suite <- function(data, vars, outcome = "SevereBinary") {
  res <- purrr::map_dfr(vars, ~ run_univariate_test(data, .x, outcome = outcome))
  if (nrow(res) == 0) return(res)
  res %>%
    mutate(p_adj_fdr = p.adjust(p_value, method = "BH")) %>%
    arrange(p_adj_fdr, p_value)
}
```

# UNIVARIATE TEST BATTERY: Selected features + (optional) all candidate predictors
```{r}
# What: Run hypothesis tests for the LASSO-selected base variables, then optionally for all candidate predictors.
# Why: Professor wants hypothesis tests for as many variables as possible + explicit t-tests where applicable.

if (!exists("switrs_reduced")) stop("switrs_reduced not found. Run our cleaning/feature engineering first.")
if (!("SevereBinary" %in% names(switrs_reduced))) stop("SevereBinary not found in switrs_reduced.")

# --- A) Tests on SELECTED FEATURES (from our glmnet feature selection summary) ---
# feature_selection_summary chunk created: selected_vars (with column base_var)
if (exists("selected_vars") && "base_var" %in% names(selected_vars)) {
  selected_base_vars <- unique(selected_vars$base_var)
} else if (exists("train_set")) {
  # fallback: if selected_vars isn't available, at least test what’s in the model
  selected_base_vars <- setdiff(names(train_set), c("SevereBinary"))
} else {
  stop("Neither selected_vars nor train_set exists. Run glmnet feature selection (or baseline model) first.")
}

# Remove anything outcome/leakage-ish just in case
if (exists("leakage_vars")) selected_base_vars <- setdiff(selected_base_vars, leakage_vars)
selected_base_vars <- intersect(selected_base_vars, names(switrs_reduced))

cat("Selected base variables to test:", length(selected_base_vars), "\n")

tests_selected <- run_univariate_suite(switrs_reduced, selected_base_vars, outcome = "SevereBinary")

cat("\nTop 30 (FDR-adjusted) significant selected variables:\n")
print(head(tests_selected, 30))

# --- B) Optional: tests on ALL candidate predictors we considered ---
# Uses our candidate_predictors object from baseline modeling chunk if present
if (exists("candidate_predictors")) {
  all_vars <- candidate_predictors
  if (exists("leakage_vars")) all_vars <- setdiff(all_vars, leakage_vars)
  all_vars <- intersect(all_vars, names(switrs_reduced))

  cat("\nAll candidate variables to test:", length(all_vars), "\n")
  tests_all <- run_univariate_suite(switrs_reduced, all_vars, outcome = "SevereBinary")

  cat("\nTop 30 (FDR-adjusted) significant variables among ALL candidates:\n")
  print(head(tests_all, 30))
} else {
  cat("\nSkipping ALL-variable tests: candidate_predictors not found (run baseline modeling chunk first).\n")
}
```

# Re-run tests after removing "Not Stated" categories (sensitivity check)
```{r}
# What: Remove "Not Stated" as a level before hypothesis testing.
# Why: Provides cleaner inference on real categories; include as sensitivity analysis.

switrs_noNS <- switrs_reduced %>%
  dplyr::mutate(across(where(is.character), ~ dplyr::na_if(.x, "Not Stated"))) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(across(where(is.factor), ~ {
    x <- as.character(.x)
    x[x == "Not Stated"] <- NA_character_
    factor(x)
  }))

# Selected-vars rerun
tests_selected_noNS <- run_univariate_suite(switrs_noNS, selected_base_vars, outcome = "SevereBinary")

cat("\nTop 30 selected vars (NO Not Stated, FDR-adjusted):\n")
print(head(tests_selected_noNS, 30))
```

# Print contingency tables for top categorical predictors (by FDR)
```{r}
# What: Show the raw counts used by chi-square / prop tests.
# Why: Makes the hypothesis testing deliverable more “textbook” and auditable.

print_top_tables <- function(test_tbl, data, top_k = 8) {
  top_vars <- test_tbl %>%
    dplyr::filter(grepl("^categorical", type)) %>%
    dplyr::arrange(p_adj_fdr) %>%
    dplyr::pull(variable) %>%
    unique() %>%
    head(top_k)

  for (v in top_vars) {
    cat("\n=============================\n")
    cat("Contingency table for:", v, "\n")
    cat("=============================\n")

    # KEEP EXACTLY the same table construction you already used:
    tab <- table(
      normalize_not_stated(data[[v]], blank_to_notstated = TRUE),
      data$SevereBinary,
      useNA = "no"
    )

    # FIX ONLY THE LABELS (do NOT change counts/levels):
    if (exists("legend_maps") && v %in% names(legend_maps) && exists("lookup_meaning")) {
      rn <- rownames(tab)
      rownames(tab) <- lookup_meaning(rn, legend_maps[[v]])
    }

    print(addmargins(tab))
  }
}

if (exists("tests_selected") && nrow(tests_selected) > 0) {
  print_top_tables(tests_selected, switrs_reduced, top_k = 8)
}
```

# Comparison and operating point metrics
```{r model_compare, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# MODEL COMPARISON: GLM baseline vs glmnet (AUC + threshold metrics)
# What: Put both models on the same footing and summarize test performance.
# Why: Client-ready model selection story + operating threshold guidance under class imbalance.

# Helper: compute metrics from probs at a cutoff
metric_at_cutoff <- function(y01, p, cutoff) {
  pred <- as.integer(p >= cutoff)
  tp <- sum(pred == 1 & y01 == 1)
  tn <- sum(pred == 0 & y01 == 0)
  fp <- sum(pred == 1 & y01 == 0)
  fn <- sum(pred == 0 & y01 == 1)

  data.frame(
    cutoff = cutoff,
    sensitivity = ifelse(tp + fn > 0, tp / (tp + fn), NA_real_),
    specificity = ifelse(tn + fp > 0, tn / (tn + fp), NA_real_),
    precision  = ifelse(tp + fp > 0, tp / (tp + fp), NA_real_),
    accuracy   = (tp + tn) / (tp + tn + fp + fn),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
}

# Need: test_set + test_phat from GLM chunk; p_test + y_test from glmnet chunk
if (!exists("test_set")) stop("test_set not found (from GLM baseline chunk).")
if (!exists("test_phat")) stop("test_phat not found (from GLM baseline chunk).")
if (!exists("p_test")) stop("p_test not found (from glmnet chunk).")
if (!exists("y_test")) stop("y_test not found (from glmnet chunk).")

# GLM test outcome/probs
y_glm <- as.integer(test_set$SevereBinary == "Severe/Fatal")
p_glm <- as.numeric(test_phat)

# glmnet test outcome/probs
y_en <- as.integer(y_test)   # already 0/1
p_en <- as.numeric(p_test)

# AUCs
if (!requireNamespace("pROC", quietly = TRUE)) install.packages("pROC")
auc_glm <- NA_real_
auc_en  <- NA_real_

if (requireNamespace("pROC", quietly = TRUE)) {
  auc_glm <- as.numeric(pROC::auc(pROC::roc(y_glm, p_glm, quiet = TRUE)))
  auc_en  <- as.numeric(pROC::auc(pROC::roc(y_en,  p_en,  quiet = TRUE)))
}

# Prevalence cutoff based on training set
prev <- mean(as.integer(train_set$SevereBinary == "Severe/Fatal"))

# Metrics at 0.50 and prevalence cutoffs
glm_05   <- metric_at_cutoff(y_glm, p_glm, 0.5)
glm_prev <- metric_at_cutoff(y_glm, p_glm, prev)

en_05    <- metric_at_cutoff(y_en,  p_en,  0.5)
en_prev  <- metric_at_cutoff(y_en,  p_en,  prev)

glm_05$model <- "GLM";     glm_prev$model <- "GLM"
en_05$model  <- "glmnet";  en_prev$model  <- "glmnet"

perf_tbl <- dplyr::bind_rows(glm_05, glm_prev, en_05, en_prev) %>%
  dplyr::select(model, cutoff, sensitivity, specificity, precision, accuracy)

auc_tbl <- data.frame(
  model = c("GLM", "glmnet"),
  test_auc = c(auc_glm, auc_en)
)

cat("Test AUC comparison:\n")
print(auc_tbl)

cat("\nOperating point metrics (test set):\n")
print(perf_tbl)
```

# EXECUTIVE SUMMARY (Storyboard-ready text)
```{r exec_summary, results='asis'}
# EXECUTIVE SUMMARY (Storyboard-ready text)
# What: Print a concise narrative tying hotspots + scenarios + hypothesis tests + model performance.
# Why: Gives paste-ready language for storyboard + final report in client-friendly terms.

cat("## Executive summary\n\n")

# ---- Key model performance ----
auc_glm_val    <- NA_real_
auc_glmnet_val <- NA_real_
if (exists("auc_tbl") && all(c("model","test_auc") %in% names(auc_tbl))) {
  auc_glm_val    <- auc_tbl$test_auc[auc_tbl$model == "GLM"]
  auc_glmnet_val <- auc_tbl$test_auc[auc_tbl$model == "glmnet"]
}

# ---- Weekend vs weekday quick difference (for narrative) ----
weekend_diff_pp <- NA_real_
if ("weekend" %in% names(switrs_reduced)) {
  tmp <- switrs_reduced %>%
    dplyr::filter(!is.na(SevereBinary), !is.na(weekend)) %>%
    dplyr::group_by(weekend) %>%
    dplyr::summarise(p = mean(SevereBinary == "Severe/Fatal"), n = dplyr::n(), .groups="drop")
  if (all(c("Weekend","Weekday") %in% tmp$weekend)) {
    weekend_diff_pp <- 100 * (tmp$p[tmp$weekend=="Weekend"] - tmp$p[tmp$weekend=="Weekday"])
  }
}

# ---- Pull top hotspot + top scenario for concrete callouts ----
top_hotspot <- NA_character_
top_hotspot_n <- NA_integer_
if (exists("hotspots_by_count") && nrow(hotspots_by_count) > 0) {
  top_hotspot <- hotspots_by_count$location_key[1]
  top_hotspot_n <- hotspots_by_count$severe_n[1]
}

top_scenario <- NA_character_
top_scenario_rate <- NA_real_
if (exists("top_by_rate") && nrow(top_by_rate) > 0) {
  top_scenario <- top_by_rate$scenario[1]
  top_scenario_rate <- top_by_rate$severe_per_100[1]
}

# ---- Interaction quick callouts (Yes group rates) ----
interaction_callouts <- NULL
interaction_vars <- intersect(c("night_dui","rain_speed","ped_dark"), names(switrs_reduced))
if (length(interaction_vars) > 0) {
  interaction_callouts <- purrr::map_chr(interaction_vars, function(v) {
    df <- switrs_reduced %>%
      dplyr::filter(!is.na(SevereBinary)) %>%
      dplyr::mutate(g = as.character(.data[[v]])) %>%
      dplyr::filter(g %in% c("Yes","No")) %>%
      dplyr::group_by(g) %>%
      dplyr::summarise(rate = 100*mean(SevereBinary=="Severe/Fatal"), n = dplyr::n(), .groups="drop")
    yes_row <- df[df$g=="Yes", ]
    if (nrow(yes_row) == 0) return(NULL)

    label <- dplyr::recode(v,
      night_dui  = "Night × DUI proxy",
      rain_speed = "Rain × Unsafe speed proxy",
      ped_dark   = "Pedestrian × Dark",
      .default = v
    )
    paste0(label, ": ",
           round(yes_row$rate[1], 2), " per 100 (n=", yes_row$n[1], ")")
  })
  interaction_callouts <- interaction_callouts[!is.na(interaction_callouts)]
}

# ---- Narrative ----
cat(
  "We analyzed crash-level SWITRS records (one row per CASE_ID) to identify **where** and **under what conditions** severe/fatal crashes are most concentrated.\n\n"
)

if (!is.na(top_hotspot)) {
  cat(sprintf(
    "- **Where (burden):** The highest severe/fatal *count* hotspot is **%s** (%s severe/fatal crashes).\n",
    top_hotspot, top_hotspot_n
  ))
}

if (!is.na(top_scenario)) {
  cat(sprintf(
    "- **When/conditions (risk intensity):** The highest severe/fatal *rate* scenario is **%s** (%.2f severe/fatal per 100 crashes).\n",
    top_scenario, top_scenario_rate
  ))
}

if (!is.na(weekend_diff_pp)) {
  cat(sprintf(
    "- **Weekend effect:** Severe/fatal risk is about **%.2f percentage points** higher on weekends than weekdays (difference in proportions).\n",
    weekend_diff_pp
  ))
}

if (!is.null(interaction_callouts) && length(interaction_callouts) > 0) {
  cat("\n**Compound risk signals (interaction terms):**\n")
  for (s in interaction_callouts) cat("- ", s, "\n", sep = "")
}

cat("\n")

cat(sprintf(
  "**Predictive validation:** A baseline logistic regression achieves test AUC ≈ %s, and a penalized logistic regression (glmnet) achieves test AUC ≈ %s. We prefer glmnet for stability with many categorical levels.\n\n",
  ifelse(is.na(auc_glm_val), "N/A", format(round(auc_glm_val, 3), nsmall = 3)),
  ifelse(is.na(auc_glmnet_val), "N/A", format(round(auc_glmnet_val, 3), nsmall = 3))
))

cat("### Recommended actions\n")
cat("- **Deploy/enforce at the highest-burden hotspots** (top severe/fatal counts) for maximum near-term impact.\n")
cat("- **Prioritize the highest-risk scenarios** (top severe/fatal rates) for targeted operations (visibility, enforcement windows).\n")
cat("- **Use compound-risk conditions** (e.g., night×DUI, rain×speed proxies, pedestrian×dark) to design precision interventions.\n")
```

# model diagnostics: class imabalance and missingness
```{r}
# What: Report class balance and remaining missingness in the modeling frame used for GLM baseline.
# Why: Confirms the outcome imbalance and verifies preprocessing eliminated NA.

# Use the finalized modeling frame from Chunk 7 if present; otherwise fall back
md <- NULL
if (exists("model_df_clean2")) md <- model_df_clean2
if (is.null(md) && exists("model_df_clean")) md <- model_df_clean
if (is.null(md)) stop("No modeling frame found. Run the baseline modeling chunk first.")

cat("Class balance (modeling frame):\n")
print(table(md$SevereBinary))

cat("\nRemaining NA% by column (should be ~0 after preprocessing):\n")
remaining_na <- sort(colMeans(is.na(md)) * 100, decreasing = TRUE)
print(head(remaining_na, 20))
```

# next-step hooks (merged data expansion): “Note: PCF_VIOL_CATEGORY may cause separation in unpenalized GLM; glmnet handles it.”
```{r}
# What: List additional merged-only candidate predictors for future iterations (non-leakage).
# Why: Keeps the roadmap aligned with modeling integrity (avoid injury/death proxies).

pattern <- "PARTY_|DRIVER|VEH|SOBRIETY|DRUG|SAFETY|EQUIP|AT_FAULT|AGE|SEX|VIOL"

cands <- names(switrs_reduced)[
  grepl(pattern, names(switrs_reduced), ignore.case = TRUE)
]

# Drop known leakage / outcome-adjacent fields
leak_like <- grepl("KILLED|FATAL|INJUR|SEVER|KSI|COUNT_", cands, ignore.case = TRUE)
cands <- cands[!leak_like]

cat("Candidate merged-only predictors to consider next (non-leakage):\n")
print(head(cands, 50))
```
